title: 10 Pounds
description: The front shows Al-Rifai Mosque in Cairo with Pharaoh Khafre. This is the 2025-issued variant of the long-running P-73 type with the 2022–2026 signature period.
body: The 10-pound note issued by the Central Bank of Egypt in 2025 is a continuation of the long-running P-73 series, which has been in production across multiple signature periods since the 1990s. The obverse displays the Al-Rifa'i Mosque, a landmark of Cairo's Islamic Cairo district. The reverse features the diorite statue of Pharaoh Khafre accompanied by the falcon god Horus. The note is printed in violet, brown, red, and yellow tones on cotton paper measuring 150 × 70 mm. Security features include a solid security thread, a windowed demetallized security thread with denomination, an Eye of Horus see-through registration device, and a watermark of King Khafre with electrotype numeral 10.

highlights.reason: Ongoing circulation issue continuing the long-running P-73 definitive series with updated date and signature.
highlights.design: Obverse: Al-Rifa'i Mosque, Cairo. Reverse: Diorite statue of Pharaoh Khafre with the Horus falcon.
highlights.security: Solid and windowed demetallized security thread, Eye of Horus registration device, Khafre watermark with electrotype 10.

title: 20 Zlotych
description: The front features Bolesław Chrobry based on a 19th-century etching. It is a commemorative issue for the millennium of his coronation, with details from the Gniezno bronze doors.
body: Issued on 5 November 2025 by the Narodowy Bank Polski (NBP), this commemorative collector banknote marks the millennium of the coronation of Bolesław I the Brave, Poland's first king, in 1025. The obverse depicts Bolesław Chrobry based on a graphic by Rudolf Fryderyk Friedlein (c. 1857) inspired by a portrait by Marcello Bacciarelli. The reverse draws on the bronze Gniezno Cathedral Doors, featuring scenes of Saint Adalbert's body and the Lance of Saint Maurice. Security features include a crown-shaped window cut, a gold-and-silver security stripe, microtext on both sides, and UV-reactive scenes. The note measures 170 × 85 mm — the largest in NBP's collector series — with a mintage capped at 70,000 pieces.

highlights.reason: Commemorates the 1,000th anniversary of Bolesław I the Brave's coronation in 1025, Poland's first royal crowning.
highlights.design: Obverse: Bolesław Chrobry after 19th-century etching. Reverse: Scenes from the Gniezno bronze doors, Princes Polonie coin, Lance of Saint Maurice.
highlights.security: Crown-shaped window cut, gold-and-silver security stripe with door details, microtext, UV-reactive imagery, watermark of Gniezno Door fragment.

title: 100 Cents
description: The front shows a stylized Chinese dragon with clouds and red lanterns. It is a non-circulating numismatic note for the Chinese Lunar Year of the Dragon.
body: The Reserve Bank of Fiji issued its 100-cent numismatic polymer banknote on 8 August 2023 to celebrate the Chinese Lunar Year of the Dragon (2024). Produced by De La Rue on Safeguard® polymer substrate, it measures 145 × 70 mm. The reverse shows a stylized Chinese dragon surrounded by white and yellow clouds, accompanied by a red lantern and the Chinese character for 'dragon,' with the inscription 'Good luck in the year of the dragon.' The obverse features a red hibiscus flower, the Fijian coat of arms, and a color-shifting hibiscus security device. A holographic foil strip incorporates wave patterns, the numeral 100, a Fiji Banded Iguana, and coral motifs. Limited to 950,000 pieces.

highlights.reason: Non-circulating numismatic issue celebrating the Chinese Lunar Year of the Dragon (2024), targeting collector and Asian numismatic markets.
highlights.design: Reverse: Stylized Chinese dragon with clouds, red lantern, and 'good luck' inscription. Obverse: Hibiscus flower, Fijian coat of arms.
highlights.security: De La Rue Safeguard® polymer substrate, holographic foil strip with wildlife motifs, Spark® color-shifting OVI hibiscus device.

title: 2 Dollars
description: The front shows Philip Goldson with the national coat of arms and Antelope Falls. A distinctive feature is the waterfall theme tied to Belizean natural landmarks.
body: The Central Bank of Belize revealed its new banknote family on 23 January 2025, with the series entering circulation on 4 August 2025. The redesigned $2 note is the first Belizean currency not to feature a British monarch as its primary portrait, replacing Queen Elizabeth II with Hon. Philip Stanley Wilberforce Goldson — the longest-serving member of the Belize House of Representatives, the first Leader of the Opposition, and the first Belizean statesman to address the United Nations. Goldson was posthumously awarded the Order of the National Hero. The obverse also displays the national coat of arms and an image of Antelope Falls at Mayflower Bocawina National Park; the reverse shows Sapodilla Falls at Elijio Panti National Park.

highlights.reason: Part of a full redesign of Belizean currency replacing the British monarch with national heroes; first Belize note to feature exclusively Belizean figures.
highlights.design: Obverse: Philip Goldson portrait, national coat of arms, Antelope Falls (Mayflower Bocawina National Park). Reverse: Sapodilla Falls (Elijio Panti National Park).
highlights.security: PUREIMAGE™ holographic security thread, Enhanced GEMINI™ UV patterns, enhanced watermarks, color-shifting security thread.

title: 5 Dollars
description: The front shows George Cadle Price with the national coat of arms and the National Assembly building in Belmopan. It is distinguished by the civic and historic architecture references on the note.
body: The redesigned Belize $5 note, unveiled on 23 January 2025 and entering circulation on 4 August 2025, honors Rt. Hon. George Cadle Price — Belize's first Prime Minister, founder of the People's United Party, and recipient of the Order of the National Hero. Price led Belize to independence in 1981. The red-toned note (140 × 70 mm) displays Price's portrait alongside the national coat of arms and the National Assembly building at Independence Plaza in Belmopan on the obverse. The reverse depicts the Belize City Peninsula and Haulover Creek, the Supreme Court building, and Saint John's Cathedral, the oldest Anglican church in Central America.

highlights.reason: Part of the 2025 Belizean currency redesign celebrating national heroes; honors George Cadle Price as the founding father of independent Belize.
highlights.design: Obverse: George Cadle Price portrait, national coat of arms, National Assembly building in Belmopan. Reverse: Belize City Peninsula, Haulover Creek, Supreme Court, Saint John's Cathedral.
highlights.security: PUREIMAGE™ holographic security thread, Enhanced GEMINI™ UV patterns, enhanced watermarks, color-shifting security thread.

title: 10 Dollars
description: The front shows Philip Goldson with the national coat of arms and a green heron. Its distinctive theme is Belizean birdlife, echoed by the toucan and hummingbirds referenced on the note.
body: The Central Bank of Belize unveiled its new family of banknotes on 23 January 2025, with the designs entering circulation on 4 August 2025. The 10-dollar note (Pick B333a, dated 1 February 2025) is printed in green on paper by De La Rue and measures 140 × 70 mm. The obverse features Order of National Hero Philip Goldson alongside the national coat of arms and a green heron, with the denomination as a see-through registration device. The reverse celebrates Belizean birdlife with a keel-billed toucan, a long-billed hermit hummingbird, and a scaly-breasted hummingbird. Security features include a holographic foil stripe bearing the denomination, a windowed security thread with demetallised elements, and a portrait watermark of Philip Goldson with electrotype '10'.

highlights.reason: Series modernisation to celebrate Belize's national heritage and incorporate enhanced anti-counterfeiting technology; honours Order of National Hero Philip Goldson
highlights.design: Obverse: Philip Goldson, coat of arms, green heron; Reverse: keel-billed toucan, long-billed hermit hummingbird, scaly-breasted hummingbird
highlights.security: Holographic foil stripe; windowed security thread with demetallised elements; portrait watermark with electrotype denomination

title: 20 Dollars
description: The front shows George Cadle Price with the national coat of arms and Baird's tapir. A distinctive element is the wildlife focus, especially the tapir and jaguar imagery.
body: Part of the Central Bank of Belize's 2025 series revealed on 23 January 2025 and introduced into circulation on 4 August 2025, the 20-dollar note (Pick B334a, dated 1 February 2025) is printed in brown on paper by De La Rue and measures 140 × 70 mm. The obverse portrays Order of National Hero and first Prime Minister George Cadle Price alongside the national coat of arms and a Baird's tapir, Belize's national animal. The reverse highlights jungle wildlife: a jaguar, a green iguana, and a blue morpho butterfly. Security features include a holographic foil stripe with denomination and butterfly motif, a windowed security thread with jaguar imagery, and a watermark of George Cadle Price with electrotype '20'.

highlights.reason: Series modernisation honouring first Prime Minister George Cadle Price and promoting Belizean national identity and wildlife
highlights.design: Obverse: George Cadle Price, coat of arms, Baird's tapir; Reverse: jaguar, green iguana, blue morpho butterfly
highlights.security: Holographic foil stripe with butterfly; windowed thread with jaguar head and demetallised '20'; portrait watermark with electrotype denomination

title: 50 Dollars
description: The front shows Philip Goldson with the national coat of arms and the Mask Temple at Lamanai. It is distinguished by its strong Maya archaeological theme.
body: The 50-dollar note (Pick B335a, dated 1 February 2025) is part of the Central Bank of Belize's redesigned series unveiled 23 January 2025 and circulated from 4 August 2025. Printed in burgundy on paper by De La Rue and measuring 150 × 75 mm, it focuses on Belize's Mayan archaeological heritage. The obverse features Order of National Hero Philip Goldson, the national coat of arms, and the iconic Mask Temple at the Lamanai archaeological site in northern Belize. The reverse showcases further Mayan monuments: the summit of Caana at Caracol, El Castillo at Xunantunich, and a segment of the Xunantunich east frieze. Security features include a holographic foil stripe with denomination and the Mask Temple, a windowed thread bearing '50' and the Mask Temple, and a portrait watermark of Philip Goldson with electrotype '50'.

highlights.reason: Series modernisation celebrating Belize's Mayan archaeological legacy and honouring national hero Philip Goldson
highlights.design: Obverse: Philip Goldson, coat of arms, Mask Temple at Lamanai; Reverse: Caana ruins at Caracol, El Castillo at Xunantunich, Xunantunich east frieze
highlights.security: Holographic foil stripe with Mask Temple; windowed thread with Mask Temple and '50'; portrait watermark with electrotype denomination

title: 100 Dollars
description: The front shows George Cadle Price with the national coat of arms and a queen triggerfish. A distinctive feature is the marine theme connected to the Great Blue Hole and reef life.
body: The 100-dollar note (Pick B336a, dated 1 February 2025) is the highest denomination in the Central Bank of Belize's 2025 series, unveiled 23 January 2025 and placed into circulation on 4 August 2025. Printed in blue on paper by De La Rue and measuring 150 × 75 mm, it celebrates Belize's marine biodiversity. The obverse shows Order of National Hero George Cadle Price, the national coat of arms, and a queen triggerfish (Balistes vetula), Belize's national fish. The reverse depicts the Belize Barrier Reef ecosystem: a hawksbill sea turtle, the famous Great Blue Hole, French angelfish, a nurse shark, and grooved brain coral. Security features include an IGNITE® windowed security thread, a holographic foil stripe with the hawksbill sea turtle, and a portrait watermark of George Cadle Price.

highlights.reason: Series modernisation highlighting Belize's UNESCO-listed Barrier Reef marine ecosystem and honouring national hero George Cadle Price
highlights.design: Obverse: George Cadle Price, coat of arms, queen triggerfish; Reverse: Great Blue Hole, hawksbill sea turtle, French angelfish, nurse shark, grooved brain coral
highlights.security: IGNITE® windowed security thread; holographic foil stripe with hawksbill sea turtle; portrait watermark with electrotype denomination

title: 1,000 Pesos
description: The front shows Hermila Galindo, Carmen Serdán, and Francisco I. Madero. This commemorative variant adds the "100 Aniversario 1925–2025" overprint for the 100th anniversary of Banco de México.
body: The Banco de México 1,000-peso Series G banknote (Pick B718, predominantly grey, 153 × 65 mm, cotton substrate) was originally introduced into circulation on 19 November 2020 to commemorate the centenary of the Mexican Revolution of 1910. It is the first Mexican banknote to feature three portrait subjects simultaneously: feminist and revolutionary journalist Hermila Galindo, political activist Carmen Serdán, and President Francisco I. Madero, accompanied by an engraving of a locomotive. The reverse depicts the UNESCO-recognised natural and cultural heritage site of Calakmul in Campeche. Security features include RAPID® micro-optic thread with kinetic 3D effects, Sparklive® optically variable numerals, intaglio printing, and Multicode® thread.

highlights.reason: Commemorates the centenary of the Mexican Revolution of 1910, honouring its democratic and equality ideals through the figures of Hermila Galindo, Carmen Serdán, and Francisco I. Madero; first Mexican banknote with three portrait subjects
highlights.design: Obverse: Hermila Galindo, Carmen Serdán, Francisco I. Madero, and a revolutionary-era locomotive; Reverse: Calakmul rainforest with jaguar, ceiba, and zapote trees
highlights.security: RAPID® micro-optic security thread with 3D movement; Sparklive® colour-shifting numerals; intaglio printing; Multicode® thread; watermark; fluorescent inks

issuer: Centrale Bank van Curaçao en Sint Maarten
title: 10 Caribbean Guilders
description: The front shows a gray angelfish and the Klein Curaçao lighthouse. Its distinctive feature is the marine-and-lighthouse identity of the new Caribbean guilder series.
body: The 10 Caribbean Guilder note entered circulation on 31 March 2025, replacing the Netherlands Antillean Guilder. Predominantly yellow and measuring 147 x 66 mm, the vertical front depicts a gray angelfish alongside a Queen Conch shell, celebrating the marine biodiversity of the Leeward islands. The horizontal reverse shows the iconic lighthouse on Klein Curacao, the uninhabited islet southeast of Curacao. Security features include a MOTION SURFACE stripe, a RAPID HD Detect micro-optic thread, a compass watermark, SPARK Flow color-shifting ink, and raised intaglio lines for visually impaired users. Printed on cotton paper by Crane Currency, the series was developed with input from CARMABI and the Sint Maarten Nature Foundation.

highlights.reason: Replacement of obsolete Netherlands Antillean Guilder following the 2010 monetary union between Curacao and Sint Maarten.
highlights.design: Vertical front with gray angelfish and Queen Conch shell on yellow background; horizontal reverse with Klein Curacao lighthouse.
highlights.security: MOTION SURFACE 3D stripe, RAPID HD Detect micro-optic thread, SPARK Flow color-shifting shell, compass+XCG watermark, see-through register, raised intaglio tactile lines.

issuer: Centrale Bank van Curaçao en Sint Maarten
title: 20 Caribbean Guilders
description: The front shows a spotted eagle ray. A distinctive feature is the Sint Maarten coastal setting of Simpson Bay in the new Caribbean guilder series.
body: The 20 Caribbean Guilder note, predominantly blue and issued on 31 March 2025, features two spotted eagle rays on the vertical front alongside a Yellow Cowry shell. The spotted eagle ray, common in Caribbean waters, represents the shared sea between Curacao and Sint Maarten. The horizontal reverse depicts Simpson Bay Lagoon in Sint Maarten, one of the largest inland bays in the Caribbean. All notes measure 147 x 66 mm and are printed on cotton paper by Crane Currency. The note replaces the Netherlands Antillean Guilder, legal tender since before the 2010 dissolution of the Netherlands Antilles.

highlights.reason: Part of the inaugural Caribbean Guilder series replacing the Netherlands Antillean Guilder.
highlights.design: Vertical front with two spotted eagle rays and Yellow Cowry shell on blue background; horizontal reverse with Simpson Bay Lagoon, Sint Maarten.
highlights.security: MOTION SURFACE 3D stripe, RAPID HD Detect micro-optic thread, SPARK Flow color-shifting shell, compass+XCG watermark, see-through register, raised intaglio tactile lines.

issuer: Centrale Bank van Curaçao en Sint Maarten
title: 50 Caribbean Guilders
description: The front shows a green sea turtle. Its distinctive element is the Curaçao seascape at Grote Knip, reinforcing the marine identity of the series.
body: The 50 Caribbean Guilder note, issued on 31 March 2025, is the green denomination in the series. Its vertical front portrays a green sea turtle alongside a Favored Tellin shell. The horizontal reverse showcases Playa Kenepa Grandi (Grote Knip), one of Curacao's most celebrated beaches. The series was designed in collaboration with CARMABI (Curacao) and the Sint Maarten Nature Foundation, which each received a conservation donation from the CBCS.

highlights.reason: First-ever Caribbean Guilder series replacing the Netherlands Antillean Guilder, designed to highlight the shared marine environment with a conservation message.
highlights.design: Vertical front with green sea turtle and Favored Tellin shell on green background; horizontal reverse with Playa Kenepa Grandi, Curacao.
highlights.security: MOTION SURFACE 3D stripe, RAPID HD Detect micro-optic thread, SPARK Flow color-shifting shell, compass+XCG watermark, see-through register, raised intaglio tactile lines.

issuer: Centrale Bank van Curaçao en Sint Maarten
title: 100 Caribbean Guilders
description: The front shows a stoplight parrotfish. A distinctive feature is the Court House in Sint Maarten, linking marine life with island civic heritage.
body: The 100 Caribbean Guilder note, the red denomination in the inaugural series issued on 31 March 2025, displays the stoplight parrotfish together with a Common Music Volute shell on the vertical front. The horizontal reverse features the Courthouse of Sint Maarten in Philipsburg. All notes share dimensions of 147 x 66 mm and are printed by Crane Currency. The series introduced a vertical front orientation, a first for this monetary union.

title: 200 Caribbean Guilders
description: The front shows a longsnout seahorse. It is distinguished by the Queen Emma Bridge motif, tying the note to Curaçao's best-known landmark.
body: The 200 Caribbean Guilder is the purple highest denomination in the inaugural series, issued on 31 March 2025. Its vertical front features two longsnout seahorses alongside a Giant Tun shell. The horizontal reverse showcases the Queen Emma Bridge in Willemstad, Curacao, the renowned pontoon swing bridge connecting the Punda and Otrobanda districts. The notes are pegged to the US dollar at a rate of 1 USD = 1.79 XCG.

highlights.reason: Highest denomination in the Caribbean Guilder series, replacing the Netherlands Antillean Guilder for the Curacao-Sint Maarten monetary union.

title: 5 Pounds
description: The front shows the Mosque of Ahmad Ibn Tulun in Cairo. A distinctive element is the Nile bounty frieze paired with the 2025-dated continuation of this long-running type.
body:
The 5 Egyptian Pounds note, issued since 2020 by the Central Bank of Egypt, features the Ahmad Ibn Tulun Mosque on its obverse. This mosque, completed in 879 AD, stands as Egypt's oldest intact mosque and Cairo's largest by land area at 26,300 square meters. Built under Abbasid governor Ahmad Ibn Tulun, it exemplifies 9th-century Islamic architecture with its distinctive brick-based construction, geometric stucco patterns, and spiral minaret inspired by Samarra designs. The reverse depicts a Pharaonic frieze titled "Bounty of River Nile," illustrating agricultural scenes and the Nile god Hapi with offerings of lotus and papyrus flowers, symbolizing Egypt's historical dependence on the Nile's annual flooding.

The note measures 145 x 70 mm and uses a striking combination of slate blue, green, yellow, and orange colors. The 2025-dated variant maintains this definitive paper design with consistent iconographic elements across denominations. Security features include a 1.5-mm solid security thread with demetallized denomination in Arabic, a 4-mm windowed security thread displaying "5" and "Five Pounds" in Arabic with the national emblem coat of arms, an Eye of Horus registration device, and a watermark featuring King Tuthmosis with electrotype denomination.

highlights.reason: Historic 9th-century mosque and Egypt's oldest intact religious structure, UNESCO World Heritage Site
highlights.design: Ahmad Ibn Tulun Mosque obverse; Pharaonic frieze depicting Nile god and agricultural bounty on reverse
highlights.security: Windowed security thread with demetallized text and Eye of Horus registration device

title: 20 Pounds
description: The front shows the Mosque of Muhammad Ali in Cairo. Its distinctive historical motif is the war chariot and frieze from the chapel of Senusret I.
body:
The 20 Egyptian Pounds note exists in both paper (P-74) and polymer (P-82) formats. The paper definitive version, issued by the Central Bank of Egypt since 2016, features Muhammad Ali Mosque (also called the Alabaster Mosque) on its obverse. Built 1832-1857 within Cairo's Citadel by Muhammad Ali Pasha, this Ottoman-style mosque features a dramatic central dome and semi-domes, with alabaster cladding that creates a distinctive translucent glow throughout the day. The reverse depicts Pharaonic heritage with Ramesses II in a war chariot, ceremonial scenes from Karnak temple including pharaonic coronations and temple inauguration rituals, alongside stylized papyrus flowers and the Eye of Horus.

The paper note measures 155 x 70 mm in green, purple, pink, and brown. The 2025-dated variant represents the continuing paper series alongside polymer production initiated in June 2023. The polymer 20 Pounds (P-82, green) is the first Egyptian denomination in polymer material, featuring tactile emboss technology for visually impaired accessibility. Both formats remain valid circulation currency per Central Bank of Egypt statements. Security features on the paper note include a 1.5-mm solid security thread with demetallized denomination, a 4-mm windowed thread at left with Arabic text and coat of arms, and a watermark of Princess Nofret with electrotype.

highlights.reason: Ottoman-style architectural jewel dominating Cairo's skyline; Muhammad Ali Pasha's tomb within the mosque
highlights.design: Muhammad Ali (Alabaster) Mosque obverse; Ramesses II war chariot and Karnak temple ceremonies on reverse
highlights.security: Windowed security thread with coat of arms emblem; paper and polymer variants both circulating

title: 50 Pounds
description: The front shows Abu Hariba Mosque in Cairo. A distinctive feature is the reference to Edfu Temple on this 2025-dated variant.
body:
The 50 Egyptian Pounds note features the Abu Hariba Mosque on its obverse, a Mamluk structure built 1479-1481 AD that exemplifies 15th-century Islamic architectural sophistication. The reverse displays the Edfu Temple, one of Egypt's best-preserved Ptolemaic monuments dedicated to the falcon-headed god Horus, constructed between 237 and 57 BC. The temple represents nearly two centuries of uninterrupted craftsmanship and stands 79 meters in length with 36-meter-high pylons, showcasing the pinnacle of Ptolemaic temple design with its hypostyle halls, forecourt, and sanctuary.

The 50 Pounds note measures 160 x 70 mm and uses black, brown, blue, and purple colorways. The obverse displays the mosque in maroon print against multicolored background. The 2025-dated variant maintains the definitive paper design featuring these dual Islamic and Pharaonic architectural monuments. Advanced security features include two security strips: a narrow strip to the right of the mosque with repeated "50 جنيها" visible only under ultraviolet backlight, and a thicker 4-window strip at left interwoven through the note displaying the Egyptian eagle coat of arms. Additional features include an Eye of Horus registration device and a watermark of King Akhenaten with electrotype denomination.

highlights.reason: Mamluk Islamic heritage paired with Egypt's best-preserved Ptolemaic temple spanning two dynasties
highlights.design: Abu Hariba Mosque (1479-1481 AD) on obverse; Edfu Temple of Horus (237-57 BC) on reverse with Nile boat
highlights.security: Dual security strips with color-shifting copper-brown/gold/lime-green disc; windowed security features Egyptian eagle

title: 100 Pounds
description: The front shows Sultan Hassan Mosque in Cairo. Its distinctive ancient motif is the sphinx on the matching design family.
body:
The 100 Egyptian Pounds note, part of the Central Bank of Egypt's definitive series since 2013, features the Sultan Hassan Mosque on its obverse. This grand Mamluk monument, completed in the 14th century, stands within Cairo's medieval quarter and represents Islamic architectural achievement with its soaring minarets and intricate decorative elements. The reverse displays the Great Sphinx of Giza, the 4,500-year-old limestone colossus measuring 240 feet in length and 66 feet in height, a symbol of pharaonic power and divine kingship attributed to the reign of Khafre (circa 2500 BC). The Sphinx served as guardian of the Giza necropolis and epitomizes ancient Egyptian civilization's monumental vision.

The note measures 165 x 70 mm and utilizes green, orange, purple, and brown colorways. The 2025-dated variant continues the paper definitive design linking Islamic heritage (mosque) with Egypt's most iconic Pharaonic symbol. Security features include an Eye of Horus registration device, a motion windowed security thread with dynamic anti-counterfeiting effect, a 1.5-mm solid security thread with demetallized "100 Pounds" in Arabic, and a watermark depicting the Great Sphinx with electrotype 100.

highlights.reason: Mamluk mosque paired with Egypt's most iconic 4,500-year-old Pharaonic monument symbolizing divine kingship
highlights.design: Sultan Hassan Mosque (14th century Mamluk) obverse; Great Sphinx of Giza (circa 2500 BC) reverse
highlights.security: Motion windowed security thread with dynamic anti-counterfeiting; Eye of Horus registration device

title: 200 Pounds
description: The front shows Qani Bey Mosque in Cairo. A distinctive feature is the ancient Egyptian scribe motif carried on this high-denomination design.
body:
The 200 Egyptian Pounds note features the Qani Bey Mosque on its obverse, a sophisticated Mamluk structure completed in 1503 AD (908 Hijra) in Cairo. The mosque exemplifies Mamluk architectural refinement with intricate marble inlays, geometric patterns, and ornamental carving. The reverse displays the Seated Scribe, a celebrated limestone statue from Egypt's Old Kingdom (circa 2500-2450 BC), housed in the Louvre Museum. This iconic sculpture depicts a scribe in cross-legged working position with half-rolled papyrus, inlaid rock crystal eyes, and represents a high dignitary of the Old Kingdom period, symbolizing the centrality of written administration to Pharaonic civilization.

The note measures 165 x 73 mm and employs purple, green, and brown coloration. The 2025-dated variant maintains the definitive paper design connecting Mamluk Islamic tradition (1503 AD) with ancient administrative heritage (Old Kingdom). Security features include a denomination registration device, a motion windowed security thread with dynamic effects, a 1.5-mm solid security thread with demetallized "200 Pounds" in Arabic, and a watermark depicting an Egyptian scribe with electrotype 200. The reverse cartouche contains hieroglyphic text meaning "Egypt is Peace," uniting historical themes with contemporary national aspiration.

highlights.reason: Late Mamluk architectural masterpiece linked to Old Kingdom symbol of Egyptian bureaucracy and literacy
highlights.design: Qani Bey Mosque (1503 AD, 908 Hijra) obverse; Seated Scribe Old Kingdom statue reverse with peace cartouche
highlights.security: Motion windowed security thread; watermark depicts Egyptian scribe; registration device and demetallized elements

title: 5 Pounds
description: The front shows Charles III with the Falkland Islands outline, coat of arms, and black-browed albatrosses. A distinctive feature is the polymer wildlife-and-island identity of the new series.
body: The Government of the Falkland Islands issued its £5 polymer note on Falklands Day, 14 August 2025 — the first major redesign in over 40 years and the first series to feature King Charles III. Produced by De La Rue on SAFEGUARD® polymer substrate, the red-coloured note shows Charles III on the obverse alongside black-browed albatrosses and a king penguin, with the Falkland Islands outline and coat of arms. The reverse highlights the islands' wildlife, depicting five penguin species, the national flower pale maiden, and Steeple Jason Island. Security features include a transparent king penguin window with iridescent ink, ultraviolet and infrared inks, and embossed tactile dots for visually impaired users.

highlights.reason: Transition from Queen Elizabeth II portrait to King Charles III; first redesign in over 40 years
highlights.design: Obverse: King Charles III, black-browed albatrosses, king penguin, Falkland Islands outline and coat of arms. Reverse: five penguin species, pale maiden flower, Steeple Jason Island
highlights.security: SAFEGUARD® polymer substrate, transparent king penguin window with iridescent ink, UV and infrared inks, embossed tactile dots

title: 10 Pounds
description: The front shows Charles III with the Falkland Islands outline, coat of arms, and black-browed albatrosses. A distinctive feature is the polymer wildlife-and-island identity of the new series.
body: The £10 polymer note was introduced alongside the £5 and £20 on Falklands Day, 14 August 2025, forming the complete new Charles III series. Like all three denominations, it is printed by De La Rue on SAFEGUARD® polymer and shares the common obverse layout: a portrait of King Charles III, the Falkland Islands outline, coat of arms, and local seabirds. The green-coloured note replaces older paper issues bearing Queen Elizabeth II and will circulate in parallel with them until the legacy notes are withdrawn in 2026. Security features mirror the family standard: polymer substrate, transparent penguin window, iridescent ink, UV and infrared elements, and tactile embossing.

highlights.reason: Part of the 2025 Charles III series replacing Queen Elizabeth II notes; first redesign in over 40 years
highlights.design: Obverse: King Charles III, king penguin emblem, Falkland Islands outline and coat of arms. Reverse: pale maiden flower, Steeple Jason Island, multiple penguin species. Colour: green
highlights.security: SAFEGUARD® polymer substrate, transparent king penguin window, iridescent ink, UV and infrared inks, tactile embossed dots

title: 20 Pounds
description: The front shows Charles III with the Falkland Islands outline, coat of arms, and black-browed albatrosses. A distinctive feature is the polymer wildlife-and-island identity of the new series.
body: The £20 polymer note completed the new Falkland Islands Charles III family launched on 14 August 2025 (Falklands Day). Printed by De La Rue on SAFEGUARD® polymer, the brown-coloured note carries the standard obverse design: King Charles III portrait, the Falkland Islands outline, coat of arms, and local wildlife. The reverse shows Steeple Jason Island and five penguin species native to the islands. The series marks the first note redesign in more than 40 years and transitions royal portraiture from Queen Elizabeth II to Charles III. The old paper notes remain legal tender but are scheduled for withdrawal in 2026. Security features include the polymer substrate, transparent penguin window, iridescent ink, UV/infrared printing, and tactile embossed dots.

highlights.reason: Completes the 2025 Charles III series replacing Queen Elizabeth II notes; first redesign in over 40 years
highlights.design: Obverse: King Charles III, pale maiden flower motif, Falkland Islands outline and coat of arms. Reverse: Steeple Jason Island, five penguin species. Colour: brown
highlights.security: SAFEGUARD® polymer substrate, transparent penguin window, iridescent ink, UV and infrared inks, tactile embossed dots

title: 100 Quetzales
description: The front shows Francisco Marroquín. It commemorates the anniversaries of Banco de Guatemala and San Carlos de Borromeo University.
body: Banco de Guatemala presented this commemorative 100-quetzal note on 18 May 2026, with general circulation beginning 25 May 2026, printed by Polish state security printer PWPW S.A. on 100% cotton paper in a run of 15 million pieces. The note simultaneously marks three anniversaries: 100 years of central banking in Guatemala (monetary reform of 1926), 80 years since Banco de Guatemala's founding (1946), and 350 years since the establishment of Universidad de San Carlos de Borromeo (1676). The obverse features a portrait of Bishop Francisco Marroquín (1478–1563), Guatemala's first bishop, together with a flying quetzal bird. The reverse depicts the historic building of the Universidad de San Carlos. Security features include a windowed security thread with colour-shift from green to blue, Mayan microtext, watermark of Marroquín, Braille tactile elements, latent imagery, and optically variable ink.

highlights.reason: Commemorates 100 years of central banking in Guatemala, 80th anniversary of Banco de Guatemala (1946), and 350th anniversary of Universidad de San Carlos de Borromeo (1676)
highlights.design: Obverse: Bishop Francisco Marroquín, flying quetzal bird. Reverse: historic Universidad de San Carlos de Borromeo building
highlights.security: Windowed colour-shift security thread (green to blue) with Great Jaguar pyramid, Mayan microtext, watermark, Braille tactile dots, latent imagery, optically variable ink

title: 100 Pesos
description: The front shows Juana Inés de la Cruz with the Colegio de San Ildefonso. A distinctive feature is the "100 Aniversario 1925–2025" overprint for the centenary of Banco de México.
body: Banco de México put the commemorative 100-peso Series G into circulation on 25 August 2025 to mark the central bank's centenary (1925–2025). The note is an overprinted variant of the existing polymer 100-peso bill with the addition of '100 Aniversario / 1925–2025' beneath the Banco de México monogram on the obverse. The front portrays Sor Juana Inés de la Cruz, the most celebrated poet and writer of New Hispanic literature, alongside the main courtyard of the Antiguo Colegio de San Ildefonso in Mexico City's Historic Centre. The red vertical note measures 132 × 65 mm. The reverse depicts pine, oak, and oyamel trees of the Monarch Butterfly Biosphere Reserve, a UNESCO World Heritage site.

highlights.reason: Commemorates the 100th anniversary of Banco de México (1925–2025); limited overprint on the existing polymer 100-peso note
highlights.design: Obverse: Sor Juana Inés de la Cruz portrait, courtyard of Antiguo Colegio de San Ildefonso, '100 Aniversario 1925–2025' overprint. Reverse: Monarch Butterfly Biosphere Reserve (pine, oak and oyamel forest). Red, vertical format, 132 × 65 mm
highlights.security: Polymer substrate, transparent window, SPARK Live gold-to-green colour-shifting ink, intaglio tactile printing, multicolour denomination, fluorescent ink

title: 200 Pesos
description: The front shows Miguel Hidalgo, José María Morelos, and the liberty bell motif. A distinctive feature is the "100 Aniversario 1925–2025" overprint for the centenary of Banco de México.
body:
Banco de México issued a commemorative 200 peso banknote on March 12, 2025, bearing the legend "100 Aniversario 1925-2025" to celebrate the bank's centennial. The obverse features prominent portraits of Miguel Hidalgo y Costilla and José María Morelos y Pavón, heroes of Mexican Independence, accompanied by the symbolic Bell of Dolores (Campana de Dolores). The reverse displays a majestic golden eagle in flight over the landscape of El Pinacate Biosphere Reserve and the Great Desert of Altar in Sonora, representing Mexico's diverse ecosystems.

This commemorative variant maintains the same dimensions and colour scheme as the standard G-series 200 peso note (predominantly green on a cotton substrate) while adding the centennial overprint legend below the Banco de México monogram on the obverse. The note entered circulation in March 2025 as legal tender and coexists with regular banknotes of the same denomination.

highlights.reason: Celebrates Banco de México's 100th anniversary (1925-2025) with a commemorative overprint
highlights.design: Miguel Hidalgo and José María Morelos on obverse; golden eagle over El Pinacate landscape on reverse
highlights.security: Features watermark of the Bell of Dolores, color-shifting holographic strip, and tactile lines for accessibility

title: 500 Pesos
description: The front shows Benito Juárez entering Mexico City alongside his portrait. A distinctive feature is the "100 Aniversario 1925–2025" overprint for the centenary of Banco de México.
body:
Banco de México released a commemorative 500 peso banknote bearing the "100 Aniversario 1925-2025" overprint legend to mark the institution's centennial celebration. The obverse features the portrait of Benito Juárez (1806-1872), one of Mexico's most revered historical figures, with the commemorative legend displayed below the Banco de México monogram. The reverse depicts a gray whale with its calf among seagrass, representing the El Vizcaíno Biosphere Reserve located in Baja California Sur.

This commemorative version maintains the G-series standard design specifications and color palette while incorporating the centennial designation. The 500 peso denomination was among the first to receive the commemorative overprint in 2025, and these notes are legal tender, coexisting with regular circulation notes of the same value throughout the Mexican monetary system.

highlights.reason: Commemorates Banco de México's 100th anniversary (1925-2025) with special overprint legend
highlights.design: Benito Juárez portrait on obverse; gray whale with calf in El Vizcaíno Biosphere Reserve on reverse
highlights.security: Incorporates standard G-series security features including watermark, security threads, and anti-counterfeiting elements

title: 200 Pesos
description: The front shows Miguel Hidalgo, José María Morelos, and the liberty bell motif. This is the 2025-dated continuation of the established 200-peso family rather than the centenary overprint type.
body:
Banco de México continued the circulation of its G-series 200 peso banknote into July 2025 as part of regular monetary production. The note features the same design elements established in the G-series: the obverse displays portraits of Miguel Hidalgo y Costilla and José María Morelos y Pavón, Mexican independence heroes, alongside the Bell of Dolores. The reverse showcases a golden eagle in flight over the El Pinacate Biosphere Reserve and Great Desert of Altar in Sonora.

This regular 2025-dated variant differs from the commemorative March 2025 issue by the absence of the "100 Aniversario 1925-2025" overprint legend. The banknote measures 139 mm by 65 mm, is printed on cotton fiber substrate with a predominant green coloration, and incorporates comprehensive security features including watermarks, color-shifting elements, and accessibility features for the visually impaired.

highlights.reason: Regular G-series continuation issued throughout 2025 without commemorative overprint
highlights.design: Miguel Hidalgo and José María Morelos on obverse; golden eagle over El Pinacate landscape on reverse
highlights.security: Watermark of Bell of Dolores, holographic color-shifting vertical strip, multicolor denomination numerals, and haptic identification lines

title: 10 Rufiyaa
description: The front shows men and women playing traditional drums. A distinctive feature is the cultural scene with a toddy tapper climbing a coconut palm.
body:
The Maldives Monetary Authority issued a revised 10 rufiyaa polymer banknote dated July 31, 2025, introduced into circulation on April 1, 2026. This note features the traditional imagery of Maldivian cultural heritage: the obverse displays a group of people playing bodu beru (traditional drums) in the background with a toddy tapper climbing a coconut palm tree in the foreground, representing important aspects of local livelihoods and cultural practices. The reverse features the oldest drum in the Maldives.

A significant change in this 2025-2026 variant is the inclusion of the official Rufiyaa currency symbol (ރ) preceding the denomination numerals on both sides of the note, marking the first generally circulating Maldivian banknotes to display this recently adopted symbol. The note is printed on advanced polymer substrate by De La Rue and carries the signature of MMA Governor Ahmed Munawar.

highlights.reason: First widespread circulation of 10 rufiyaa notes featuring the new official Rufiyaa currency symbol (ރ)
highlights.design: Bodu beru drummers and toddy tapper on obverse; oldest Maldivian drum featured on reverse
highlights.security: Watermark of multi-toned portrait with numeral 10, embedded metallic security thread with microtext "10 MVR", raised design elements, and color-changing features

title: 20 Rufiyaa
description: The front shows a jet taking off from Ibrahim Nasir International Airport. A distinctive feature is the maritime economy imagery with tuna fishing and the traditional dhoni.
body:
The Maldives Monetary Authority released a revised 20 rufiyaa polymer banknote dated July 31, 2025, entering circulation on February 17, 2026. The obverse features maritime economy imagery including a fisherman carrying skipjack and yellowfin tuna catch, a cowry shell (Cyprea moneta) historically significant in Maldivian commerce, and a jet airplane at takeoff from Ibrahim Nasir International Airport (now officially called Velana International Airport). The reverse displays complementary maritime and economic themes.

The primary distinguishing feature of this 2025-2026 edition is the addition of the official Rufiyaa currency symbol (ރ) before the denomination numerals, making these among the first widely circulated notes to incorporate the newly adopted symbol. Printed on polymer substrate by De La Rue, the note measures 150 mm by 70 mm with a green coloration and carries the signature of MMA Governor Ahmed Munawar.

highlights.reason: Introduces newly adopted Rufiyaa currency symbol (ރ) on widely circulating banknotes; features airport imagery marking infrastructure expansion
highlights.design: Fisherman with tuna catch and airport jet on obverse; maritime economy and cowry shell imagery
highlights.security: Watermark of multi-toned portrait with numeral 20, embedded metallic security thread with microtext, raised design elements, and advanced polymer substrate anti-counterfeiting measures

title: 100 Rufiyaa
description: The front shows a group in traditional attire, including a woman working on a libaas neckline. A distinctive feature is the early Dhivehi script reference on the note.
body: The Maldives Monetary Authority issued this 100-rufiyaa polymer note (catalog B227b) bearing the date 31 July 2025 and the signature of Governor Ahmed Munawar. It is a signature-and-date update to the existing polymer family first introduced in January 2025. The obverse depicts a seated woman in traditional Libaas dress performing Hiru — the intricate neckline-threading craft — capturing Maldivian artisanal heritage. The reverse shows the Dambidu Lomafanu, an early Dhivehi palm-leaf scripture. The note is printed on De La Rue Safeguard polymer substrate and retains the full prior security architecture.

highlights.reason: Routine signature-and-date update to introduce the officially adopted rufiyaa currency symbol across the polymer banknote family.
highlights.design: Obverse: seated woman in Libaas traditional dress performing Hiru neckline threading; reverse: Dambidu Lomafanu early Dhivehi palm-leaf scripture.
highlights.security: De La Rue Safeguard polymer substrate with intaglio raised print and optically variable elements.

title: 100,000 Tögrög
description: The front shows Chinggis Khaan against a mountainous backdrop. It commemorates the 100th anniversaries of the People's Revolution and the tögrög currency.
body: Mongolbank introduced this 100,000-tögrög commemorative note on 9 December 2025, marking a dual centenary: the 100th anniversary of the People's Revolution of 1921 and the 100th anniversary of the tögrög, which entered circulation on 9 December 1925. The obverse features Chinggis Khaan against a mountainous backdrop alongside the Soyombo national symbol and a flaming green-to-gold SPARK security element. The reverse shows the Founding Seven of the People's Revolution including Damdin Sukhbaatar, plus a map of Mongolia. The note is printed in orange, brown, red, and blue.

highlights.reason: Dual centenary commemorative: 100th anniversary of the Mongolian People's Revolution and 100th anniversary of the introduction of the tögrög (9 December 1925).
highlights.design: Obverse: Chinggis Khaan, Soyombo symbol, mountain range, warrior-on-horseback registration device; reverse: portraits of the Founding Seven of the People's Revolution, map of Mongolia.
highlights.security: Flaming green-to-gold SPARK optically variable element; windowed security thread with demetalized text.

title: 10 Zlotych
description: The front shows Duke Mieszko I. A distinctive feature is the 2025-dated refresh of the standard note with the silver denar motif.
body: Narodowy Bank Polski issued this updated 10-złoty circulation note (catalog B859c) bearing the date 30 January 2025 and new signatures of Governor Glapiński and Treasurer Jaroszek. It is a standard periodic refresh of the existing Royal Piast dynasty series in circulation since 1995, with no redesign of the artwork. The obverse depicts Duke Mieszko I, Poland's first historically documented ruler, flanked by rosettes from Gniezno Cathedral. The reverse shows a silver denar from Mieszko I's reign, framed by stylised columns from the Benedictine Abbey in Tyniec. The note measures 120×60 mm in dark brown and green.

highlights.reason: Routine circulation series refresh: new date (30 January 2025) and updated governor/treasurer signatures, no change in design.
highlights.design: Obverse: portrait of Duke Mieszko I with Gniezno Cathedral rosettes; reverse: Mieszko I silver denar flanked by Benedictine Abbey columns.
highlights.security: Watermark (crown in oval); intaglio raised print; security thread; colour-shifting ink.

title: 20 Zlotych
description: The front shows King Bolesław I Chrobry. A distinctive feature is the 2025-dated refresh of the standard note with the medieval seal motif.
body: Narodowy Bank Polski released a 20-złoty collector commemorative note (catalog BNP829a) on 5 November 2025, celebrating the 1,000th anniversary of the coronation of Bolesław I Chrobry. A circulation date-and-signature variant (B860c, dated 19 March 2025) was also confirmed. The collector note features a portrait bust of Bolesław resting on a sword hilt with an eagle-and-shield motif on the obverse. The reverse reproduces scenes from the famous Gniezno Doors: the ransom of St. Adalbert's body, its procession, and burial at Gniezno. A crown-shaped die-cut window is punched through the note. Limited to 70,000 pieces, it was named Best Commemorative Banknote of 2025 by the International Bank Note Society.

highlights.reason: 1,000th anniversary of the coronation of Bolesław I Chrobry as Poland's first king (1025–2025); circulation date variant also exists (19 March 2025).
highlights.design: Obverse: portrait bust of Bolesław Chrobry with sword hilt and eagle-on-shield; reverse: Gniezno Door scenes; crown-shaped die-cut window.
highlights.security: Crown-shaped die-cut window through the paper; intaglio raised print; watermark; security thread.

title: 2,000 Rupees
description: The front shows the Central Bank of Sri Lanka building in Colombo. It commemorates the bank's 75th anniversary under the motto "Stability for Prosperity."
body: The Central Bank of Sri Lanka (CBSL) issued this Rs. 2,000 circulated commemorative note on 29 August 2025 to mark its 75th anniversary (1950–2025), themed 'Stability for Prosperity'. It is the fifth commemorative currency note in CBSL history and enters general circulation with a planned print run of 50 million pieces. The obverse features the Central Bank headquarters building in Colombo, the Colombo Lighthouse Clock Tower, and the Colombo skyline silhouette alongside the 75th anniversary logo. The reverse displays a stylised map of Sri Lanka, a water lily, the lion holding a sword from the national coat of arms, and the anniversary logo. The note measures 151×67 mm in a predominantly blue colour scheme.

highlights.reason: 75th anniversary of the Central Bank of Sri Lanka (established 1950), under the theme 'Stability for Prosperity'; fifth commemorative note in CBSL's history.
highlights.design: Obverse: CBSL headquarters, Colombo Lighthouse Clock Tower, Colombo skyline, 75th anniversary logo; reverse: stylised map of Sri Lanka, water lily, lion-and-sword motif, anniversary logo.
highlights.security: Wide colour-shifting security strip with image of Colombo Clock Tower; watermark of lion holding sword; 100% coated cotton paper substrate.

title: 50 Dinars
description: The front shows Hédi Nouira. A distinctive feature is the Banque Centrale de Tunisie building in Tunis on this 2025-dated issue.
body:
The 50 Dinars banknote from Tunisia's Banque Centrale de Tunisie was issued in July 2025 with new signatures (Abdessalem/Nouri) and an official date of 25.07.2025. The front features Hédi Nouira, the first governor of the Central Bank of Tunisia, positioned at center with Arabic denomination markings in circular design.

The reverse depicts the headquarters building of the Banque Centrale de Tunisie in Tunis as the primary architectural focal point, alongside symbolic representations and French text indicating the central bank's authority and Tunisia's monetary system.

The note measures 158 x 79 mm and is printed on polymer paper. Security features include a 5.0-mm wide Rapid HD windowed security thread with demetalized denomination, and a watermark of Hédi Nouira with electrotype denomination marking.

highlights.reason: Monetary redesign and reissue with updated signatures and July 2025 date reflecting current central bank officials.
highlights.design: Portrait of Hédi Nouira on front; Banque Centrale de Tunisie headquarters building in Tunis on reverse.
highlights.security: Rapid HD windowed security thread with demetalized 50 and Hédi Nouira watermark with electrotype.

title: 20 Dinars
description: The front shows Farhat Hached. A distinctive feature is the El Jem amphitheater on this 2025-dated version.
body:
The 20 Dinars banknote from Tunisia's Banque Centrale de Tunisie was reissued in July 2025 with new signatures (Abdessalem/Nouri) and official date 25.07.2025. The front displays the portrait of Farhat Hached, a prominent Tunisian nationalist leader and labor activist, centered with Arabic inscription and red color scheme with gold denomination marking.

The reverse features an aerial view and detailed close-up of the Roman amphitheater of El Jem, one of the most significant archaeological monuments in Tunisia and a UNESCO World Heritage Site. A Corinthian column is also depicted alongside French text.

The security features include a windowed security thread and watermark of Farhat Hached. The note represents Tunisia's commitment to honoring historical figures while celebrating its rich architectural and cultural heritage.

highlights.reason: Updated monetary issue with new signatures and July 2025 date reflecting current central bank governance.
highlights.design: Portrait of Farhat Hached on front; Roman amphitheater of El Jem with Corinthian column on reverse.
highlights.security: Windowed security thread and watermark of Farhat Hached.

title: 5 Dinars
description: The front shows Slaheddine el-Amami. A distinctive feature is the Roman aqueducts of Zaghouan on the matching design.
body:
The 5 Dinars banknote from Tunisia's Banque Centrale de Tunisie was issued in July 2025 with new signatures (Abdessalem/Nouri) and official date 25.07.2025. The front features agronomist Slaheddine el-Amami, a significant figure in Tunisian agricultural science and development, with green-to-blue SPARK Live color-shifting denomination marking.

The reverse displays the Roman aqueducts of Zaghouan, an engineering marvel dating to antiquity that supplied water to the ancient city of Carthage. These monumental structures are depicted with French inscriptions highlighting Tunisia's ancient Roman heritage.

The note measures 143 x 73 mm on polymer paper. Security features include a 4.0-mm wide Rapid HD windowed security thread with demetalized denomination, and a watermark of Slaheddine el-Amami. The SPARK Live element provides color change from green to blue with dynamic moving rings visible at different viewing angles.

highlights.reason: New July 2025 issue with updated signatures reflecting current central bank leadership and monetary policy continuity.
highlights.design: Portrait of agronomist Slaheddine el-Amami on front; Roman aqueducts of Zaghouan on reverse.
highlights.security: Rapid HD windowed thread, SPARK Live color-shifting element, and Slaheddine el-Amami watermark.

title: 10 Dinars
description: The front shows Dr. Tawhida Ben Cheikh. A distinctive feature is the Berber pottery and jewellery theme on this 2025-dated issue.
body:
The 10 Dinars banknote from Tunisia's Banque Centrale de Tunisie was issued in July 2025 with new signatures (Abdessalem/Nouri) and official date 25.07.2025. The front features Dr. Tawhida Ben Cheikh (1909-2010), Tunisia's first female medical doctor and a pioneering gynecologist, shown with green-to-blue SPARK Live denomination marking.

The reverse displays traditional Berber pottery and jewelry, representing Tunisia's rich indigenous cultural heritage and craftsmanship. These artistic elements highlight the importance of traditional Berber arts in Tunisian society.

The note features a light blue, green, and orange color scheme. Security features include a windowed security thread and Dr. Tawhida Ben Cheikh watermark. The SPARK Live element provides dynamic color-shifting effects from green to blue.

highlights.reason: July 2025 reissue with updated signatures honoring Dr. Tawhida Ben Cheikh as a symbol of women's achievement in medicine and science.
highlights.design: Portrait of Dr. Tawhida Ben Cheikh on front; Berber pottery and jewelry on reverse.
highlights.security: Windowed security thread with SPARK Live color-shifting element and Dr. Tawhida Ben Cheikh watermark.

title: 200 Bolívares
description: The front shows three portraits of Simón Bolívar. It commemorates the bicentennial of the Battle of Maracaibo.
body:
The 200 Bolívares banknote from Venezuela's Banco Central de Venezuela commemorates the bicentennial of the naval Battle of Lake Maracaibo (1823-2023). The note was introduced in August 2024 and reissued in June 2025 with new signatures (Guerra/Pérez) and date 12.06.2025.

The front features three distinct portraits of Simón Bolívar representing different periods of his life and leadership, demonstrating the evolution of the renowned South American liberator. The note is predominantly purple, yellow, and black in color scheme.

The reverse depicts the Puente Rafael Urdaneta, a cable-stayed bridge at the Tablazo Strait outlet of Lake Maracaibo, alongside imagery of the Naval Battle of Lake Maracaibo—a pivotal 1823 naval engagement where Gran Colombian forces defeated the Spanish Empire. The note includes a watermark of Simón Bolívar and security features consistent with modern Venezuelan currency standards.

highlights.reason: Commemorates the 200-year anniversary (1823-2023) of the naval Battle of Lake Maracaibo, a crucial event in South American independence.
highlights.design: Three portraits of Simón Bolívar on front; Puente Rafael Urdaneta bridge and naval battle scene on reverse.
highlights.security: 2-mm windowed security thread with demetalized BCV, Simón Bolívar watermark, and optically variable ink.

title: 500 Bolívares
description: The front shows three portraits of Simón Bolívar. It commemorates the bicentennial of the Battle of Maracaibo.
body:
The 500 Bolívares banknote from Venezuela's Banco Central de Venezuela commemorates the bicentennial of the naval Battle of Lake Maracaibo (1823-2023). The note was introduced in August 2024 and reissued in June 2025 with new signatures (Guerra/Pérez) and date 12.06.2025.

The front showcases three distinct portraits of Simón Bolívar representing different eras of his historic leadership and influence across South America, rendered in orange, brown, and green color scheme. This multi-portrait design emphasizes Bolívar's enduring significance.

The reverse displays the Puente Rafael Urdaneta, a major cable-stayed bridge at the Tablazo Strait outlet of Lake Maracaibo, combined with detailed imagery of the Naval Battle of Lake Maracaibo—the pivotal July 24, 1823 naval engagement that secured independence from Spanish colonial rule. The note includes Simón Bolívar watermark and advanced security features including optically variable ink on the denomination numeral.

highlights.reason: Bicentennial commemorative for the 1823 naval Battle of Lake Maracaibo, foundational to Venezuelan and South American independence.
highlights.design: Three portraits of Simón Bolívar on front; Puente Rafael Urdaneta and naval battle imagery on reverse.
highlights.security: 2-mm windowed security thread with demetalized BCV, Simón Bolívar watermark, and optically variable ink with color shift.

title: 50 Kina
description: Arms and Parliament building at Port Moresby with anniversary context for 50 years of independence and national currency.
body:
Papua New Guinea issued this commemorative 50 Kina polymer banknote on August 28, 2025, marking the 50th anniversary of the nation's independence (September 16, 1975) and the 50th anniversary of the national currency. The note enters circulation and is legal tender alongside special numismatic packaging for collectors.

The obverse features Papua New Guinea's coat of arms (the bird of paradise perched on the Kundu drum and ceremonial spear), the National Parliament building in Port Moresby, the Raggiana Bird of Paradise (the national bird), Queen Alexandra's butterfly (the world's largest), and the Blue Orchid. The 50 YEARS anniversary logo is prominently displayed on the front.

The reverse depicts Sir Michael Somare, Papua New Guinea's founding Prime Minister, alongside cultural artifacts representing the nation's diversity—spears from the Sepik region, shields and masks from New Ireland, Tubuan face masks from New Britain, headdresses from the Highlands, and masks and costumes from the Sepik River.

Manufactured by Note Printing Australia, this Guardian polymer substrate banknote measures 145 × 70 mm and contains no security thread but features a watermark of the Bank of Papua New Guinea logo. A total of 20 million commemorative notes were issued alongside 20 million 50 toea coins.

highlights.reason: Commemorates the 50th anniversary of Papua New Guinea's independence (1975-2025) and the 50th anniversary of the kina currency.
highlights.design: Orange polymer note featuring Parliament House, the national coat of arms, Raggiana Bird of Paradise, Queen Alexandra's butterfly, Blue Orchid, and Sir Michael Somare.
highlights.security: Watermark with Bank of Papua New Guinea logo; Guardian polymer substrate provides inherent security through material composition.

title: 100 Kina
description: Arms and Parliament building with a memorial issue for the first anniversary of Sir Julius Chan.
body:
The Bank of Papua New Guinea issued this commemorative 100 Kina polymer banknote in January 2026 to honor Sir Julius Chan, the nation's founding finance minister, second Prime Minister, and father of the Kina and Toea currency. The obverse features the National Parliament building in Port Moresby alongside the national coat of arms depicting a bird of paradise perched on a Kundu drum and ceremonial spear.

The reverse introduces a portrait of Sir Julius Chan accompanied by symbols of Papua New Guinea's maritime trading heritage: a New Ireland shark caller, a lakatoi sailing vessel, a toea armband and a kina breast ornament, representing the commerce networks that predate the modern currency.

Manufactured on Guardian polymer substrate by Note Printing Australia, the note measures 150 × 70 mm and incorporates a watermark-style feature with the bank logo, a security thread, and tactile elements for accessibility. It entered circulation on 28 January 2026 and is legal tender alongside the existing denominations.

highlights.reason: Commemorates the first anniversary of the passing of Sir Julius Chan, founding finance minister and architect of the Kina.
highlights.design: Front: Parliament House and national coat of arms; back: Sir Julius Chan with shark caller, lakatoi and traditional ornaments.
highlights.security: Guardian polymer substrate with bank-logo watermark feature, security thread, and tactile elements for vision-impaired users.

title: 100 Somoni
description: Ismoili Somoni on the front with the 25 Years of Somoni currency commemorative issue.
body:
The National Bank of Tajikistan issued this commemorative 100 Somoni banknote on October 30, 2025, to celebrate the 25th anniversary of the introduction of the somoni as Tajikistan's national currency (2000-2025). This represents the first commemorative paper currency in the history of independent Tajikistan and was officially recognized as the best commemorative banknote in the world at the international High Security Printing competition in 2025.

The obverse features the portrait of Ismoili Somoni (also known as Ismoil Somoni), the founder of the first centralized Tajik state during the 9th century, rendered with text in the Tajik language. The design includes six concave hexadecagons, the National Bank of Tajikistan logo as a registration device, the official 25th-anniversary emblem, the Samanid Mausoleum (the historic tomb of Ismoili Somoni in Bukhara), and the national coat of arms.

The reverse depicts the newly constructed Parliament building of Tajikistan alongside the national flag, with text in both English and Tajik languages. The paper banknote measures 156 × 65 mm with color tones in light blue, dark blue, pink, red, and green.

Produced by Giesecke & Devrient, this banknote features a 6-mm purple-orange RollingStar Venus windowed security thread with demetalized marks—the first banknote worldwide to incorporate such a thread with MotifFlip and ColorFlip optical effects.

highlights.reason: Celebrates the 25th anniversary of the somoni currency (2000-2025) and marks Tajikistan's first official commemorative banknote.
highlights.design: Features portrait of Ismoili Somoni, Samanid Mausoleum, modern Parliament building, and national coat of arms with multicolor security patches.
highlights.security: First banknote in the world with RollingStar Venus windowed security thread featuring MotifFlip and ColorFlip effects; includes demetalized marks.

title: 1 Rubl'
description: Generalissimo Alexander Suvorov with commemorative overprint for the 80th anniversary of victory in WWII.
body:
The Pridnestrovian Republican Bank (the de facto central bank of Transnistria/Pridnestrovian Moldavian Republic) issued this commemorative 1 Ruble banknote in 2025 with an overprint marking the 80th anniversary of victory in World War II (1945-2025). The commemorative banknote follows Transnistrian tradition of issuing special editions for significant historical anniversaries, applying commemorative overprints to existing notes for both general circulation and numismatic collectors.

The banknote features Generalissimo Alexander Suvorov, the celebrated 18th-century Russian military leader and the last Generalissimo of the Russian Empire. Suvorov is venerated in Transnistrian history and culture as a founding figure of military tradition and is prominently featured on Transnistrian currency.

The commemorative 2025 edition includes the 80th anniversary of victory emblem and the St. George ribbon, symbolizing the Great Patriotic War (World War II from the Soviet perspective). The note remains legal tender and was issued in both standard circulation and special numismatic packaging for collectors.

As part of Transnistria's broader 2025 commemorative currency program, this issue honors the historical significance of Allied victory in WWII while maintaining cultural continuity with the nation's 1 ruble denomination.

highlights.reason: Commemorates the 80th anniversary of victory in World War II (1945-2025) with overprint applied to standard 1 Ruble banknote.
highlights.design: Obverse features Generalissimo Alexander Suvorov with 80th anniversary emblem and St. George ribbon symbolizing WWII victory.
highlights.security: Standard security features of the regular 1 Ruble banknote with commemorative overprint for historical anniversary.

title: 100 Baht
description: King Rama X with commemorative text for the 150th anniversary of the Ministry of Finance.
body:
The Bank of Thailand issued this commemorative 100 Baht banknote on April 14, 2025, to celebrate the 150th anniversary of the founding of Thailand's Ministry of Finance (1875-2025). The note is identical in size and specifications to the regular 100 Baht banknotes of the 17th Series but is distinguished by the commemorative inscription.

The obverse features the portrait of King Maha Vajiralongkorn (Rama X, the current Thai monarch) in his official capacity, with the inscription "150 Years Ministry of Finance April 14, 2025" displayed in red on the lower front edge and in the lower portion of the note. The design maintains the established characteristics of Thailand's 100 Baht denomination while marking this significant institutional milestone.

The reverse depicts two historic Thai monarchs—King Chulalongkorn (Rama V, 1868-1910) and King Vajiravudh (Rama VI, 1910-1925)—recognizing their roles in establishing and developing the Ministry of Finance as an institution. This commemorative issue was released in special packaging that includes a matching commemorative coin of the same anniversary theme.

The banknote remains full legal tender for all transactions. The Bank of Thailand made this note available both for general circulation and through special numismatic channels, allowing collectors and the public to commemorate this institutional anniversary.

highlights.reason: Commemorates the 150th anniversary of the Ministry of Finance (1875-2025) with inscription and special packaging.
highlights.design: Obverse features King Rama X with commemorative inscription; reverse depicts King Rama V and King Rama VI honoring the ministry's historic development.
highlights.security: Maintains standard 100 Baht security features; distinguishable by red commemorative inscription "150 Years Ministry of Finance April 14, 2025".

title: 1.000 Taka
description: The front features the National Martyrs' Monument in Savar alongside shapla flowers with violet and purple hues. The reverse displays the Jatiya Sangsad Bhaban (National Parliament building) in Dhaka, part of Bangladesh's new architectural heritage series.
body: Bangladesh Bank introduced a redesigned 1000 taka note on June 1, 2025, as part of the 'Historical & Archaeological Architecture of Bangladesh' series following the 2024 political transition. The new design eliminates human portraits in favor of iconic national landmarks and cultural symbols. The obverse features the National Martyrs' Monument at Savar—a pivotal independence war memorial—flanked by shapla flowers (Bangladesh's national flower), with denomination in optically variable ink (OVI). The reverse displays the impressive Jatiya Sangsad Bhaban designed by architect Louis Kahn. The redesign reflects a broader modernization of Bangladeshi currency to enhance security and celebrate the nation's architectural heritage.

title: 5 Taka
description: Sheikh Mujibur Rahman appears on the front with the National Martyrs' Monument visible. The reverse displays the Naogaon Kusumba mosque alongside Bangladesh's national symbols including the shapla flower, rice, and jute.
body: The 5 taka note, redesigned in 2016, features a portrait of Sheikh Mujibur Rahman, the Father of the Nation and founding father of Bangladesh, positioned at the left of the obverse. The National Martyrs' Monument in Savar is depicted in light color at the center. At 117 x 60 mm, it is Bangladesh's smallest banknote. The reverse showcases the historic Naogaon Kusumba mosque, representing Bangladesh's architectural heritage, alongside the national coat of arms with symbolic elements: the shapla flower (national flower), rice, and jute (economic mainstays). The note is printed in purple and includes security features to prevent counterfeiting.

body: Bangladesh Bank issued a commemorative 200 taka note on March 18, 2020, to honor the birth centenary of Bangabandhu Sheikh Mujibur Rahman (1920-2020), the founding father and first president of Bangladesh. Though commemorative, the note is legal tender for all domestic transactions. The obverse features a prominent portrait of Sheikh Mujibur Rahman with electrotype denomination '200' and the Bangladesh Bank coat of arms. At the top, 'Father of the Nation Bangabandhu Sheikh Mujibur Rahman Centenary 1920-2020' is inscribed, with 'Birth Centenary' printed at the left corner. The reverse displays another portrait of Sheikh Mujibur Rahman against a background showing farmers on the riverbank, reflecting his commitment to agrarian development. The note measures 146 x 63 millimeters and represents Bangladesh's first 200 taka denomination.

title: 2 Dollars
description: John Redman Bovell, the Barbadian agronomist credited with saving the sugar industry, appears on the blue polymer note. The reverse features the Morgan Lewis Windmill, the last operational sugar windmill in Barbados.
body: The Central Bank of Barbados issued the 2 Dollar note in 2022 as part of its historic transition to polymer banknotes, making Barbados one of the first Caribbean nations to adopt this durable plastic substrate. The note features a portrait of John Redman Bovell (1855-1928), a pioneering scientist and agronomist whose genetic experiments with sugar cane varieties saved Barbados's vital sugar industry and established the island as a leading cane breeding station in the Caribbean. In 1908, Bovell was awarded the Imperial Service Star for his contributions to tropical agriculture. The obverse displays his portrait in blue with red elements. The reverse showcases the Morgan Lewis Windmill in St. Andrew, Barbados—the last sugar windmill to operate in the nation—representing the agricultural heritage that Bovell helped preserve. The polymer note includes advanced security features and was recognized as the 'Best New Series' at Reconnaissance International's Regional Banknote and ID Document Awards.

title: 5 Dollars

body: The Central Bank of Barbados issued the $5 note in December 2022 as part of its historic transition to polymer banknotes. The note celebrates Sir Frank Worrell (1924–1967), a cricket icon who pioneered West Indian cricket excellence alongside Sir Everton Weekes and Sir Clyde Walcott. The reverse showcases the 3Ws Oval, the cricket facility named after these three national heroes. The polymer note features advanced security technology including the De La Rue SPOTLIGHT™ holographic feature—images sharpen under mobile phone torch light and blur under diffuse lighting. This series was recognized as Best New Series at Reconnaissance International's Regional Banknote and ID Document Awards in 2023.

title: 10 Dollars
description: Right Excellent Charles Duncan O'Neal, a medical doctor and social activist who fought for equality and workers' rights in the 1920s-1930s, appears on this brown polymer note. The reverse features the Charles Duncan O'Neal Bridge spanning the Careenage in Bridgetown.
body: The Central Bank of Barbados issued the $10 note in December 2022 as part of its historic polymer banknote series. The note honors Charles Duncan O'Neal (1879–1936), one of Barbados's 10 National Heroes, who agitated against racism and for the rights of the underprivileged. He founded the Democratic League in 1924 and is remembered as a champion of social justice. The reverse features the iconic Charles Duncan O'Neal Bridge, an architectural landmark spanning the Careenage in Bridgetown. The note includes enhanced security features and a unique tactile X-shaped emboss for visually impaired users. All six denomination notes in this series create a map of Barbados when arranged sequentially.

body: The Central Bank of Barbados issued the $20 note in December 2022, honoring Samuel Jackman Prescod (1806–1871), one of Barbados's 10 National Heroes. Prescod was a pioneering political activist and journalist who used his editorship to agitate for social justice and equality. He became the first non-white member of the House of Assembly in 1843. The reverse showcases the historic Parliament Buildings. The $20 note features De La Rue's revolutionary SPOTLIGHT™ holographic stripe—a first for circulating banknotes worldwide. This interactive security feature sharpen under mobile phone torch light, providing enhanced anti-counterfeiting protection. The note includes a circle-shaped tactile emboss for accessibility.

body: The Central Bank of Barbados issued the $50 note in December 2022, celebrating Errol Barrow (1920–1987), one of Barbados's most beloved national figures. Barrow led the Democratic Labour Party to victory in 1961 and became the nation's first Prime Minister upon independence in 1966, serving until 1976. He is credited as the architect of modern Barbados. The reverse showcases Independence Square in Bridgetown with Barrow's statue, symbolizing his enduring legacy. The note features the innovative De La Rue SPOTLIGHT™ holographic stripe, providing advanced security through interactive visual effects. The L-shaped tactile emboss aids visually impaired users. When all six denominations are arranged sequentially, they collectively form a map of Barbados.

title: 100 Dollars
description: Sir Grantley Herbert Adams, Barbados's first Premier and a central figure in the nation's social revolution, appears on this gray polymer note. The reverse features Grantley Adams International Airport in Christ Church, named in his honor.
body: The Central Bank of Barbados issued the $100 note in December 2022, honoring Sir Grantley Adams (1898–1971), Barbados's first Premier and one of the nation's 10 National Heroes. Adams was instrumental in the social revolution that transformed Barbados and guided the nation's path toward independence and prosperity. The reverse features Grantley Adams International Airport in Christ Church, the nation's primary gateway and a lasting monument to his leadership. The note includes the De La Rue SPOTLIGHT™ holographic stripe with sophisticated security elements. The hologram displays dolphins, broken tridents, Pride of Barbados flowers, and the numeral '100.' A square-shaped tactile emboss provides accessibility for visually impaired users. Watermarks featuring Adams's image add further security.

title: 10 Konvertibilnih Maraka
description: Mehmedalija 'Mak' Dizdar (1917–1971), an influential Bosnian poet, appears on this Federation variant note in Latin script. The reverse features a fragment of the Stećak Radimlja, a medieval tombstone representing Bosnia's rich cultural heritage.
body: The Central Bank of Bosnia and Herzegovina issued the 10 KM note on June 22, 1998, as the currency was established following the Dayton Agreement. The Federation variant (Latin script) features Mehmedalija 'Mak' Dizdar, one of Bosnia's most celebrated 20th-century poets. The note represents the dual-entity design system of BiH banknotes, with parallel Latin-script and Cyrillic-script variants that serve both the Federation of Bosnia and Herzegovina and Republika Srpska. The reverse displays a fragment of Stećak Radimlja, medieval tombstones that symbolize Bosnia's historical and cultural significance. The note includes advanced micro-optical security elements and iridescent features. Current notes from 2012 onward remain in full circulation; pre-2012 issues ceased to be legal tender on January 1, 2025.

title: 10 Konvertibilnih Maraka
description: Aleksa Šantić (1868–1924), a celebrated Serbian and Yugoslav poet, appears on this Republika Srpska variant note in Cyrillic script. The reverse features a loaf of bread, representing Šantić's literary legacy and cultural significance.
body: The Central Bank of Bosnia and Herzegovina issued the 10 KM Cyrillic-script note on June 22, 1998, representing Republika Srpska within the dual-entity banknote system. The note features Aleksa Šantić (1868–1924), a prominent Serbian and Yugoslav poet whose works are foundational to South Slavic literature. Unlike the Federation variant with its Stećak imagery, the Republika Srpska reverse displays a loaf of bread, symbolizing cultural and poetic themes in Šantić's work. Both variants—Latin-script (Federation) and Cyrillic-script (Republika Srpska)—are fully interchangeable legal tender throughout Bosnia and Herzegovina, reflecting the country's constitutional structure and post-war reconciliation through currency design. Current 2012+ issues remain in circulation; pre-2012 notes ceased legal tender on January 1, 2025.

The Federation variant (Latin script) honors Antun Branko Šimić (1898–1925), a pioneering Croatian expressionist poet whose experimental work transformed Croatian literature. Šimić's portrait represents the Croatian cultural contribution within Bosnia and Herzegovina's multi-ethnic heritage. The reverse displays Stećak Radimlja, medieval tombstones that serve as powerful symbols of Bosnia's historical continuity and artistic legacy. body: The Central Bank of Bosnia and Herzegovina issued the 20 KM Cyrillic-script note on July 27, 1998, completing the higher-denomination set in the dual-entity banknote system. The note features Filip Višnjić (1767–1847), a legendary Serbian folk poet and singer whose epic poems are cornerstones of South Slavic oral tradition. Višnjić represents the Serbian cultural heritage within Bosnia and Herzegovina's constitutionally mandated balance between Federation and Republika Srpska.
