title: 50 Konvertibilnih Maraka
description: This 50 KM note features Musa Ćazim Ćatić (Federation variant) or Jovan Dučić (Republika Srpska variant), representing Bosniak and Serbian literary heritage respectively. The reverse features Zgošća Stećak (Federation) or books and a pen symbolizing intellectual heritage (Republika Srpska).
body: The Central Bank of Bosnia and Herzegovina issued the 50 KM note on July 27, 1998, as the highest denomination in the initial banknote release. The Federation variant features Musa Ćazim Ćatić (1878–1915), a pivotal Bosniak Muslim poet and intellectual whose literary contributions shaped modern Bosnian culture. The Republika Srpska variant features Jovan Dučić (1871–1943), a Bosnian Serbian poet and diplomat whose diplomatic efforts advanced South Slavic literature and political thought. The 50 KM reverse in the Cyrillic variant displays books, reading glasses, and a pen—symbols of literary heritage. The Federation version features Zgošća Stećak, another medieval tombstone. Both variants remain fully legal tender. Enhanced 2012+ issues are in circulation; pre-2012 notes ceased legal tender January 1, 2025.

body: Originally issued on July 27, 1998, following the Dayton Agreement that ended the Bosnian War, this 50 KM note celebrates Jovan Dučić, one of Bosnia's most celebrated literary figures. The note exists in two interchangeable variants: a Federation version (Latin script) and a Republika Srpska version (Cyrillic script), reflecting Bosnia's unique political structure. The 2012 redesign introduced the Motion™ security thread, a 4mm windowed element featuring a quill that appears to move left and right when the note is tilted. Additional security measures include an optically variable ink on the denomination number (shifting from purple-red to greenish brown), watermark with Dučić's profile, and microprinting details.

title: 100 Konvertibilnih Maraka
description: Nikola Šop (1904–1982), acclaimed Croatian-Bosnian poet, graces the obverse of this 100 KM note in the Federation variant (Latin script). The reverse features the Zgošća Stećak, a medieval monumental tombstone representing Bosnia's rich cultural heritage and ancient traditions.
body: First issued July 27, 1998, this 100 KM Federation note honors Nikola Šop, a prominent Yugoslav-era Bosnian Croat poet whose work shaped the region's literary landscape. The choice to feature the Zgošća Stećak on the reverse—an iconic medieval monumental stone—underscores Bosnia's continuous cultural identity spanning centuries. The 2012 series enhanced security with the Motion™ windowed security thread (4mm) featuring a quill that moves when tilted, creating an anti-counterfeiting visual effect. The denomination number employs optically variable ink that shifts between dark brown and greenish-gold hues depending on viewing angle. Watermark and microprinting provide additional authentication layers.

issuer: Central Bank of Bosnia and Herzegovina
title: 100 Konvertibilnih Maraka
description: Petar Kočić (1877–1916), distinguished Bosnian Serb writer, playwright, and political activist, appears on the obverse of this 100 KM note in the Republika Srpska variant (Cyrillic script). The reverse depicts literary symbols—books, pen, and reading glasses—reflecting his enduring impact on Bosnian Serb cultural and political life.
body: Issued July 27, 1998, this 100 KM Republika Srpska variant commemorates Petar Kočić, a pivotal figure in Bosnian Serb literature and nationalism who fought for Bosnian autonomy in the early 20th century. Kočić's life was cut short at age 39 during World War I, but his literary and political legacy profoundly influenced the region. The 2012 redesign introduced Motion™ security featuring a 4mm windowed security thread with a quill that appears to move when tilted. The denomination number shifts from dark brown to greenish-gold through optically variable ink technology. The note incorporates watermark imagery, microprinting, and enhanced materials to prevent counterfeiting.

highlights.security: Motion™ windowed security thread with moving quill motif, optically variable ink on denomination, watermark, microprinting

title: 20 Pula
description: Dr. Kgalemang Tumedisco Motsete (1899–1974), composer of Botswana's national anthem, appears on the red obverse of this 20 Pula note. The reverse showcases Botswana's mining heritage with the Orapa diamond mine installation and a stylized roan antelope, celebrating the nation's natural wealth.
body: First circulated on August 23, 2009, this 20 Pula note honors Dr. Kgalemang Tumedisco Motsete, whose composition of 'Fatshe Leno La Rona' (Our Land) became Botswana's national anthem. Measuring 138 × 69 mm, the note features Botswana's coat of arms with rearing zebras on the obverse. The reverse celebrates the Orapa diamond mine, one of Botswana's first major mining installations, alongside a roan antelope representing the nation's wildlife. Security includes a 12mm holographic stripe featuring the coat of arms and a color-switching kingfisher image, a StarChrome red-to-green windowed security thread with demetalized 'BOB 20' text, and raised embossing on the denomination for tactile identification. Two edge dots assist visually impaired users.

highlights.security: 12mm holographic stripe with switching kingfisher image, StarChrome windowed security thread, watermark of rearing zebra, raised embossing on denomination, two edge dots for accessibility

title: 50 Pula
description: Sir Seretse Khama (1921–1980), founding President of Botswana, is prominently featured on the obverse of this brown and yellow 50 Pula note, accompanied by a malachite kingfisher. The reverse depicts the iconic Okavango Delta swamps with a traditional mokoro and African fish eagle, symbolizing Botswana's natural treasures.
body: Circulated since August 23, 2009, this 50 Pula note celebrates Sir Seretse Khama, Botswana's revered founding President who led the nation to independence in 1966 and established democratic governance. The obverse features Khama's portrait alongside the Botswana coat of arms and a malachite kingfisher, a symbol of the nation's natural heritage. The reverse showcases the Okavango Delta, one of Africa's most pristine wilderness areas, with a poler navigating a traditional mokoro (dugout canoe) beneath an African fish eagle—representing Botswana's commitment to wildlife conservation. Security features include a StarChrome windowed security thread with demetalized 'BOB 50' text and color-shifting properties, a watermark depicting a rearing zebra, and electrotype '50' details.

highlights.security: StarChrome windowed security thread with demetalized BOB 50, watermark of rearing zebra, electrotype 50, cornerstones security element

title: 1 Brunei Dollar
description: His Majesty Sultan Hassanal Bolkiah Mu'izzaddin Waddaulah appears in formal military attire and traditional dastar on the obverse. The note features the bunga simpur (Dillenia suffruticosa), Brunei's national flower, alongside the Sultan Omar Ali Saifuddien mosque and the royal ceremonial barge (Mahligai).
body: Scheduled for circulation in the first half of 2026, this 1 Brunei Dollar note is part of the new GUARDIAN™ polymer series announced on September 22, 2025. The denomination presents Sultan Hassanal Bolkiah alongside the bunga simpur (Dillenia suffruticosa), Brunei's national flower. The obverse also features the Sultan Omar Ali Saifuddien mosque and the Mahligai (royal ceremonial barge), iconic symbols of Brunei's Islamic heritage. The GUARDIAN™ polymer substrate provides enhanced durability and waterproofing. DOMINO™ tactile marks—raised dots in a unique arrangement for this denomination—enable visually impaired users to identify the note.

highlights.design: Obverse: Portrait of Sultan Hassanal Bolkiah in military uniform and dastar, bunga simpur flower, Brunei coat of arms. Reverse: Sultan Omar Ali Saifuddien mosque, Mahligai ceremonial barge.
highlights.security: Color-shifting inks, dynamic window effects, DOMINO™ tactile marks (single dot pattern), GUARDIAN™ polymer substrate

title: 5 Brunei Dollars
description: Sultan Hassanal Bolkiah appears in military dress and dastar on the obverse of this 5 Brunei Dollar note alongside the bunga kunyit (Curcuma longa), the turmeric plant. The Lapau (Royal Ceremonial Hall) provides architectural context on the obverse design.
body: Part of the 2026 polymer banknote series announced in September 2025, the 5 Brunei Dollar note continues the tradition of honoring Sultan Hassanal Bolkiah while introducing a different native Brunei flower. The bunga kunyit (Curcuma longa), commonly known as turmeric, represents the nation's botanical diversity and its historical significance in traditional medicine and culinary practices throughout Southeast Asia. The Lapau, Brunei's Royal Ceremonial Hall, appears on the obverse as an important venue for state functions and cultural events. GUARDIAN™ polymer construction ensures durability in tropical climates. Color-shifting inks and dynamic window security elements provide authentication, while DOMINO™ tactile embossed marks in a multi-dot pattern unique to this denomination enable accessibility.

title: 10 Brunei Dollars
description: Sultan Hassanal Bolkiah in formal military attire and traditional dastar is featured alongside the rancah rancah (Cosmos caudatus), an ornamental flowering plant. The impressive Jame' Asr Hassanil Bolkiah mosque in Bandar Seri Begawan dominates the reverse, showcasing Brunei's Islamic heritage.
body: Part of the 2026 GUARDIAN™ polymer series, the 10 Brunei Dollar note presents Sultan Hassanal Bolkiah alongside the rancah rancah (Cosmos caudatus), a flowering plant valued for both ornamental and culinary purposes. The Jame' Asr Hassanil Bolkiah mosque, one of Southeast Asia's largest and most spectacular mosque structures completed in 1994 and named after the Sultan, is featured on this denomination. GUARDIAN™ polymer construction ensures durability in Brunei's tropical climate. Color-shifting inks and dynamic window security elements provide authentication, while DOMINO™ tactile marks in a distinctive three-dot pattern arrangement enable visually impaired identification.

title: 100 Brunei Dollars
description: Sultan Hassanal Bolkiah appears in formal military dress and dastar on the obverse, accompanied by the kuduk-kuduk (Melastoma malabathricum), a striking blue-purple flowering plant native to Brunei. The Jame' Asr Hassanil Bolkiah mosque reinforces Islamic themes on the reverse.
body: As part of the 2026 GUARDIAN™ polymer series, the 100 Brunei Dollar note features Sultan Hassanal Bolkiah alongside the kuduk-kuduk (Melastoma malabathricum), known as Singapore rhododendron, with striking blue-purple blooms that hold medicinal significance in traditional Southeast Asian medicine. The Jame' Asr Hassanil Bolkiah mosque appears again on this denomination as a unifying design element, emphasizing Brunei's Islamic foundation. GUARDIAN™ polymer substrate provides exceptional durability for this high-circulation note, with advanced anti-counterfeiting measures including optically variable inks and sophisticated window features. DOMINO™ tactile embossed marks in a unique multi-dot arrangement specific to the 100 denomination assist visually impaired users.

title: 500 Brunei Dollars
description: Al-Marhum Sultan Haji Omar 'Ali Saifuddien Sa'adul Khairi Waddien, the former Sultan and father of the current reign, is honored on this 500 Brunei Dollar note. The obverse features the tepus kantan (Etlingera solaris), a medicinal flowering ginger plant representing Brunei's botanical heritage.
body: The highest denomination in the 2026 GUARDIAN™ polymer series, the 500 Brunei Dollar note pays tribute to Al-Marhum Sultan Haji Omar 'Ali Saifuddien Sa'adul Khairi Waddien (1914–1986), the previous Sultan and father of the current reigning Sultan. Omar 'Ali Saifuddien transformed Brunei from a protectorate into a sovereign nation and established the foundation of modern Brunei Darussalam. The note features the tepus kantan (Etlingera solaris), a flowering ginger plant valued in traditional Brunei medicine and cuisine. This denomination employs the most advanced security features in the series. GUARDIAN™ polymer construction combined with state-of-the-art anti-counterfeiting measures ensures the 500 note resists fraud and counterfeiting.

title: 20 Yuan
description: A golden dragon in flight dominates the front of this commemorative note, celebrating the Year of the Dragon 2024. The reverse features children performing a traditional dragon dance with lanterns.
body: Issued to commemorate the 2024 Lunar New Year and the Year of the Dragon, this polymer banknote was released on February 10, 2024, with approximately 100 million notes entering circulation. The obverse displays a dragon alongside the national emblem of the People's Republic of China, while the reverse depicts children performing a dragon dance with traditional lanterns in red and gold tones. The note features the denomination '20' in green SICPA SPARK ORIGIN® security ink and includes text in Mongolian, Tibetan, Uyghur, and Zhuang scripts. This is the first in a planned 12-year commemorative series celebrating each Chinese zodiac animal.

highlights.security: Green SICPA SPARK ORIGIN® denomination marking, polymer substrate, multilingual text (Mongolian, Tibetan, Uyghur, Zhuang)

title: 1 Dollar
description: The obverse features a portrait of Queen Elizabeth II alongside the coat of arms of the Cayman Islands, with angel fish and a map. The reverse depicts the Bluff on Cayman Brac and a nautilus shell.
body: Issued by the Cayman Islands Monetary Authority, the 1 Dollar note serves as the primary currency for everyday transactions throughout the Cayman Islands. The design incorporates Queen Elizabeth II as the primary portrait alongside national symbols of the island. Security features include an advanced windowed security thread, watermark of a turtle, and anti-counterfeiting measures such as microprinting, color-shifting denomination indicators, and texture elements for tactile verification by the visually impaired.

title: 25 Dollars
description: The 25 Dollar note features Queen Elizabeth II on the obverse alongside the Cayman Islands coat of arms. The banknote is part of the current circulating series issued by the Cayman Islands Monetary Authority.
body: The 25 Dollar banknote was issued in August 1999 and remains part of the current circulating currency of the Cayman Islands. All notes have been printed by De La Rue currency note printers in England and feature advanced security measures. The banknote series includes six denominations ($1, $5, $10, $25, $50, and $100) with Queen Elizabeth II portrayed on the obverse of all notes. Each denomination incorporates a security thread, watermark, and serial numbers to prevent counterfeiting.

title: 100 Pesos
description: The obverse features Carlos Manuel de Céspedes (1819–1874), a leading figure in Cuba's independence movement and first president of the Ten Years' War. The reverse depicts the Anti-Imperialist Tribune 'José Martí' in Havana.
body: Issued by the Banco Central de Cuba, the 100 Pesos note honors Carlos Manuel de Céspedes, who led Cuba's first independence war against Spanish colonialism. The 2024 edition includes updated security features with microprinted security threads bearing the text 'PATRIA O MUERTE VENCEREMOS', personalized watermarks with denomination numbers, and tactile relief for identification by the visually impaired. The note incorporates the Braille system alongside Arabic numerals and letters for accessibility. The reverse showcases the monument to the Anti-Imperialist Tribune José Martí in central Havana.

title: 500 Pesos
description: The obverse features Ignacio Agramonte y Loynáz (1841–1873), a prominent leader in Cuba's independence struggle. The reverse depicts the Constituent Assembly at Guáimaro held on April 10, 1869.
body: The 500 Pesos note, released in 2024, celebrates Ignacio Agramonte, a key military commander during Cuba's Ten Years' War and author of the constitution adopted at Guáimaro. The banknote is printed on cotton fiber paper in a predominant green color. The 2024 issue includes notes printed by both Impresos de Seguridad in Havana and Goznak in Russia, differentiated by their series markings (IG and IGA respectively). The segmented security thread is microprinted with 'BCC 500' and is readable from both sides, while three green intaglio dots near the upper right corner enable identification by the visually impaired.

title: 50 Kroner
description: The 50 Kroner note features the Sallingsund Bridge on the front with Danish bridges as the design theme. The reverse displays the Skarpsalling Pot, representing Danish prehistoric artifacts.
body: Issued by Danmarks Nationalbank, the 50 Kroner banknote is part of the 'Bridges of Denmark' series introduced in 2009. An upgraded 2009A version is entering circulation from February 2026 with enhanced security features while maintaining the original Sallingsund Bridge design on the obverse and Skarpsalling Pot on the reverse. The series interprets the theme of Danish bridges as connections between various regions and as links between past and present, as conceptualized by artist Karin Birgitte Lund. These upgraded notes precede the planned entirely new banknote series expected in 2028-2029.

title: 50 Pesos Dominicanos
description: The 50 Pesos note features the Cathedral of Santa María la Menor in Santo Domingo, the first cathedral constructed in the Americas. The reverse displays Dominican national heritage symbols.
body: The 50 Pesos Dominicanos note honors the Catedral Primada de América (Cathedral of Santa María la Menor) in Santo Domingo, the first cathedral built in the Americas, constructed between 1510 and 1540. The 2024 version continues the design tradition of celebrating Dominican architectural and cultural landmarks. The banknote incorporates multiple security features including watermarks, security threads, variable magnetic ink, and microprints to prevent counterfeiting.

title: 100 Pesos Dominicanos
description: The 100 Pesos note features three Dominican founding fathers: Francisco del Rosario Sánchez, Juan Pablo Duarte, and Matías Ramón Mella, who led the Dominican independence movement of 1844.
body: The 100 Pesos Dominicanos banknote honors the three founding fathers of the Dominican Republic: Francisco del Rosario Sánchez, Juan Pablo Duarte, and Matías Ramón Mella. These three revolutionary leaders founded the secret society 'La Trinitaria' in 1838, which culminated in Dominican independence from Haiti on February 27, 1844. The 2024 version celebrates these national heroes with their triple portrait displayed prominently on the obverse. The banknote incorporates modern security measures including watermarks, security threads, microprints, and color-shifting elements.

title: 200 Pesos Dominicanos
description: The 200 Pesos note features the three Mirabal sisters—Patria, Minerva, and María Teresa—who were prominent anti-Trujillo activists murdered for their political opposition.
body: The 200 Pesos Dominicanos banknote honors the Mirabal sisters: Patria, Minerva, and María Teresa Mirabal, who became national symbols of resistance against Dominican dictator Rafael Leónidas Trujillo. These three sisters founded the resistance movement 'Las Mariposas' (The Butterflies) and were brutally murdered on November 25, 1960. Their deaths sparked widespread international condemnation and contributed to Trujillo's eventual downfall. The 2024 banknote celebrates their legacy with their portraits prominently displayed on the obverse. The note incorporates modern security features including watermarks, security threads, microprints, and holograms.

title: 500 Pesos Dominicanos

body: The 500 Pesos Dominicanos banknote honors Salomé Ureña de Henríquez (1850–1897), revered as one of the most distinguished Dominican poets and founders of the Instituto de Señoritas, and her son Pedro Henríquez Ureña (1884–1946), a towering figure in literature and philosophy across Latin America. The 2024 version continues to celebrate their legacy with their dual portraits prominently displayed on the obverse. The reverse displays the headquarters of the Central Bank of the Dominican Republic. The banknote incorporates modern security measures including watermarks, security threads, microprints, holograms, and variable magnetic ink.

title: 1.000 Francs Guinéens
body: The 1.000 Francs Guinéens note issued by Banque Centrale de la République de Guinée in 2024 is part of the ongoing currency circulation series. This denomination facilitates transactions in Guinea's domestic economy and represents the central bank's commitment to maintaining a modern, secure currency system. The note incorporates contemporary security features including enhanced microprinting, color-shifting inks, and updated security threads to protect against counterfeiting. As a 2024 variant, this emission reflects administrative updates to printing specifications or official signatures while maintaining design consistency with previous issues in the series. The note supports both retail commerce and banking operations throughout the currency's circulation area.

title: 500 Piso
body:
The Philippine 500 Piso polymer banknote, issued in 2024 and continuing into 2025, features the Visayan spotted deer (Rusa alfredi) on its gold obverse, replacing the former portraits of President Corazon Aquino and Senator Benigno Aquino Jr. The obverse also displays the Acanthephippium mantinianum orchid. The reverse side features the Puerto Princesa Subterranean River National Park, a UNESCO World Heritage Site, along with the blue-naped parrot and southern Philippine weave design. The Visayan spotted deer is one of the world's rarest deer species, endemic to the rainforests of Panay and Negros islands.

Introduced as part of the First Philippine Polymer (FPP) Banknote Series launched on December 19, 2024, this note began circulation on December 23, 2024. The use of this critically endangered endemic species underscores the BSP's commitment to biodiversity conservation and raising public awareness of the Philippines' unique and threatened wildlife heritage.

highlights.reason: Showcases the critically endangered, endemic Visayan spotted deer to promote awareness and conservation of one of the world's rarest deer species.
highlights.design: Features Visayan spotted deer and Acanthephippium mantinianum orchid on obverse; Puerto Princesa Subterranean River National Park and blue-naped parrot on reverse.
highlights.security: Includes CCL Secure's IRISWITCH feature with gold iridescence, vertical clear window with dynamic metallic wavy lines and 3D rainbow effect, embossed tactile dots, and denomination mirroring.

title: 50 Piso
body:
The Philippine 50 Piso polymer banknote, first issued in 2024 and continuing into 2025, features the Visayan leopard cat (Prionailurus bengalensis rabori) on its obverse, replacing the former portrait of President Sergio Osmeña. The note showcases Vidal's lanutan (Hibiscus campylosiphon), an endemic flower, on the front, while the reverse retains Taal Lake and the giant trevally fish. The Visayan leopard cat, endemic to the islands of Negros, Cebu, and Panay, is depicted as a symbol of independence and agility in Filipino culture.

As part of the First Philippine Polymer (FPP) Banknote Series launched by the Bangko Sentral ng Pilipinas on December 19, 2024, this polymer note began circulation on December 23, 2024, in the Greater Manila area. The shift from presidential portraits to endangered fauna reflects the BSP's commitment to raising awareness about the country's rich biodiversity and the need for conservation of endemic species.

highlights.reason: Raises awareness of the critically endangered Visayan leopard cat and promotes conservation of Philippine endemic wildlife.
highlights.design: Features Visayan leopard cat and Vidal's lanutan flower on obverse; Taal Lake and giant trevally on reverse.
highlights.security: Includes CCL Secure's IRISWITCH feature, dynamic metallic wavy lines in vertical clear window, embossed tactile dots, and color-shifting security features with red iridescence.

title: 100 Piso
body:
The Philippine 100 Piso polymer banknote, introduced in 2024 and continuing into 2025, features the Palawan peacock-pheasant (Polyplectron napoleonis) on its violet obverse, replacing the former portrait of President Manuel A. Roxas. The front also displays the Ceratocentron fesselii, an endemic and critically endangered orchid species. The reverse side continues to feature the Mayon Volcano in Bicol and the whale shark (Rhincodon typus), accompanied by Bicol Region textile design patterns.

Launched as part of the First Philippine Polymer (FPP) Banknote Series by the Bangko Sentral ng Pilipinas on December 19, 2024, the note entered circulation on December 23, 2024. The Palawan peacock-pheasant is endemic to Palawan Island and represents the country's commitment to showcasing and protecting its unique endemic avifauna and plant species through currency design.

title: 50 Piso
body:
The Philippine 50 Piso paper banknote from the New Generation Currency (NGC) series, with 2023-2025 date variants, features President Sergio Osmeña (1878-1961), the fourth president of the Philippines from 1944 to 1946, on its red obverse. The note depicts historical elements including the First Philippine Assembly in 1907 and General Douglas MacArthur's landing in Leyte. The reverse side showcases Taal Lake (also known as Bombón Lake) in Batangas province and the giant trevally fish (Caranx ignobilis), also known locally as maliputo. The 2023 version bears the signatures of President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. and BSP Governor Felipe Medalla.

This banknote represents the New Generation Currency series, an enhanced version of Philippine paper currency with improved security features and accessibility improvements for visually impaired users, including embossed tactile marks. The note measures 160 mm x 66 mm and includes a solid security thread and watermark featuring Sergio Osmeña.

highlights.reason: Honors President Sergio Osmeña and commemorates Philippine democratic heritage through historical imagery of the First Parliament and Liberation.
highlights.design: Features Sergio Osmeña and First Parliament on obverse; Taal Lake and giant trevally (maliputo) fish on reverse.
highlights.security: Includes solid security thread, watermark of Sergio Osmeña with electrotype 50, enhanced color-changing security threads, and embossed tactile marks for visually impaired users.

title: 100 Piso
body:
The Philippine 100 Piso paper banknote from the New Generation Currency (NGC) series, with 2023-2025 date variants, features President Manuel A. Roxas on its violet obverse. The front also includes imagery of the Central Bank of the Philippines headquarters in Manila and the inauguration of the Third Republic on July 4, 1946, commemorating Roxas's presidency and role in Philippine independence. The reverse side features the Mayon Volcano in Legazpi City, Albay, a distinctive and iconic active stratovolcano, along with the whale shark (Rhincodon typus), the world's largest fish species. The reverse design also incorporates Bicol Region textile patterns.

This paper banknote version features enhanced security including a color-changing security thread added in 2020 and includes embossed tactile features for the visually impaired. The note measures 160 mm x 66 mm and incorporates a watermark of Manuel A. Roxas with electrotype 100, representing the continuation of the NGC series with modernized security features.

highlights.reason: Honors President Manuel A. Roxas and the founding of the Third Republic of the Philippines through historical and natural imagery.
highlights.design: Features Manuel A. Roxas and Third Republic imagery on obverse; Mayon Volcano and whale shark on reverse with regional textile design.
highlights.security: Includes watermark, color-changing security threads, enhanced indigenous pattern security elements, and embossed tactile marks for visually impaired accessibility.

title: 50 Piso
body:
The Philippine 50 Piso paper banknote from the New Generation Currency (NGC) series, with 2023-2025 date variants, features President Sergio Osmeña (1878-1961), the fourth president of the Philippines from 1944 to 1946, on its red obverse. The note depicts historical elements including the First Philippine Assembly in 1907 and General Douglas MacArthur's landing in Leyte. The reverse side showcases Taal Lake (also known as Bombón Lake) in Batangas province and the giant trevally fish (Caranx ignobilis), also known locally as maliputo. The 2023 version bears the signatures of President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. and BSP Governor Felipe Medalla.

This banknote represents the New Generation Currency series, an enhanced version of Philippine paper currency with improved security features and accessibility improvements for visually impaired users, including embossed tactile marks. The note measures 160 mm x 66 mm and includes a solid security thread and watermark featuring Sergio Osmeña.

highlights.reason: Honors President Sergio Osmeña and commemorates Philippine democratic heritage through historical imagery of the First Parliament and Liberation.
highlights.design: Features Sergio Osmeña and First Parliament on obverse; Taal Lake and giant trevally (maliputo) fish on reverse.
highlights.security: Includes solid security thread, watermark of Sergio Osmeña with electrotype 50, enhanced color-changing security threads, and embossed tactile marks for visually impaired users.

title: 100 Piso
body:
The Philippine 100 Piso paper banknote from the New Generation Currency (NGC) series, with 2023-2025 date variants, features President Manuel A. Roxas on its violet obverse. The front also includes imagery of the Central Bank of the Philippines headquarters in Manila and the inauguration of the Third Republic on July 4, 1946, commemorating Roxas's presidency and role in Philippine independence. The reverse side features the Mayon Volcano in Legazpi City, Albay, a distinctive and iconic active stratovolcano, along with the whale shark (Rhincodon typus), the world's largest fish species. The reverse design also incorporates Bicol Region textile patterns.

This paper banknote version features enhanced security including a color-changing security thread added in 2020 and includes embossed tactile features for the visually impaired. The note measures 160 mm x 66 mm and incorporates a watermark of Manuel A. Roxas with electrotype 100, representing the continuation of the NGC series with modernized security features.

highlights.reason: Honors President Manuel A. Roxas and the founding of the Third Republic of the Philippines through historical and natural imagery.
highlights.design: Features Manuel A. Roxas and Third Republic imagery on obverse; Mayon Volcano and whale shark on reverse with regional textile design.
highlights.security: Includes watermark, color-changing security threads, enhanced indigenous pattern security elements, and embossed tactile marks for visually impaired accessibility.

title: 2.000 Rupiah
body:
The 2,000 Rupiah note from the 2022 Bank Indonesia series features Mohammad Hoesni Thamrin, a prominent Indonesian nationalist and independence leader. The obverse displays his portrait along with Indonesia's national coat of arms and a map of the nation. The reverse showcases three elements reflecting Indonesian cultural identity: the Tari Piring, a traditional plate dance from West Sumatra, Ngarai Sianok canyon landscape near Bukittinggi, and the Jeumpa flower (champak/Magnolia champaca).

The note measures 126 mm × 65 mm with dominant colors of slate grey and violet. Printed on cotton fiber paper, it was released on 17 August 2022, bearing signatures of Governor Perry Warjiyo and Finance Minister Sri Mulyani Indrawati. The color palette shifts through blue and gray tones, offering visual distinctiveness from other denominations.

Security elements include a solid security thread with "BANK INDONESIA" text, watermark of Mohammad Hoesni Thamrin, and ultraviolet-reactive components integrated throughout the design for anti-counterfeiting protection.

highlights.reason: Honors Mohammad Hoesni Thamrin, a key nationalist leader in Indonesia's independence struggle.
highlights.design: Reverse features Tari Piring (plate dance), Ngarai Sianok canyon landscape in West Sumatra, and Jeumpa (champak) flowers.
highlights.security: Solid security thread with printed text, watermark of the featured hero, and UV fluorescence technology.

title: 1.000 Rupiah
body:
The 1,000 Rupiah banknote from the 2022 Bank Indonesia series honors Tjut Meutia (1870–1910), an Indonesian national heroine and independence fighter. The obverse features her portrait alongside the Indonesian coat of arms and a map of Indonesia. The reverse depicts three cultural and geographical elements: Fort Belgica in Banda Neira located in the Spice Islands, an orchid flower, and the Tari Tifa, a traditional dance where performers play hand drums.

Measuring 121 mm × 65 mm, the note displays colors of yellow, green, orange, purple, and red. It was issued on 17 August 2022, signed by Bank Indonesia Governor Perry Warjiyo and Finance Minister Sri Mulyani Indrawati. The banknote is printed on cotton fiber paper.

Security features include an Omron ring pattern, a solid security thread with "BANK INDONESIA" printed text, a bank logo registration device, and a watermark portraying Tjut Meutia. The 2022 series incorporates ultraviolet fluorescence and phosphorescent technology for enhanced authentication across all denominations.

highlights.reason: Commemorates Tjut Meutia, a female independence heroine of the Aceh resistance movement.
highlights.design: Reverse features Fort Belgica in Banda Neira, a Tari Tifa dancer with drum, and orchid flowers representing Indonesia's archipelago heritage.
highlights.security: Solid security thread with printed text, Omron rings, watermark of Tjut Meutia, and UV fluorescence elements.

title: 5.000 Rupiah
description: Dr. K. H. Idham Chalid, arms.
body:
The 5,000 Rupiah banknote from the 2022 series honors Dr. K.H. Idham Chalid, a prominent Islamic scholar and independence advocate. The obverse features his portrait with Indonesia's coat of arms and a tuberose flower at the bottom-left, accompanied by a map and bank logo registration device. The reverse displays a tricolor design in orange, blue, and pink tones, depicting the Tari Gambyong dancer (with traditional scarves), Mount Bromo volcano in East Java, and tuberose flowers (Polianthes tuberosa), representing Indonesia's natural beauty and cultural heritage.

Measuring 131 mm × 65 mm, the note is predominantly orange-brown and tan with dark brown printing on the multicolor underprint. Issued on 17 August 2022, it is signed by Governor Perry Warjiyo and Finance Minister Sri Mulyani Indrawati. The banknote is manufactured from cotton fiber paper for durability.

Security features include a solid security thread with printed "BANK INDONESIA" text, a watermark of Dr. K.H. Idham Chalid with electrotype 5, and ultraviolet fluorescence technology integrated throughout for authentication purposes.

highlights.reason: Commemorates Dr. K.H. Idham Chalid, a respected Islamic scholar and supporter of Indonesian independence.
highlights.design: Reverse depicts Tari Gambyong traditional scarf dancer, Mount Bromo volcano of East Java, and tuberose flowers.
highlights.security: Solid security thread with text, watermark of the honoree with electrotype, and UV fluorescence elements.

title: 10.000 Rupiah
body:
The 10,000 Rupiah note from the 2022 Bank Indonesia series features Frans Kaisiepo, a Papuan national hero and political leader who championed Papua's integration into the Indonesian republic. The obverse displays his portrait alongside Indonesia's Garuda Pancasila coat of arms, a map, the Cempaka Hutan Kasar flower, and Asmat Papua traditional motifs reflecting indigenous cultural heritage. The reverse showcases the Tari Pakarena, a traditional masked fan dance from South Sulawesi, combined with Wakatobi National Park scenery and Cempaka Hutan Kasar flowers.

The banknote measures 135–136 mm × 65 mm in predominantly purple, orange, and pink colors. Issued on 17 August 2022, it bears the signatures of Governor Perry Warjiyo and Finance Minister Sri Mulyani Indrawati, printed on cotton fiber paper. The 2022 series incorporates advanced security features including Rapid HD® micro lenses and OVMI Spark® technology on larger denominations for enhanced anti-counterfeiting capabilities.

Security elements include a solid security thread, watermark of Frans Kaisiepo, and both overt ultraviolet fluorescence and covert phosphorescent technology throughout the design for comprehensive authentication protection.

highlights.reason: Honors Frans Kaisiepo, a Papuan national hero and champion of Indonesian unity and regional integration.
highlights.design: Reverse features Tari Pakarena masked fan dance from South Sulawesi, Wakatobi National Park landscape, and Cempaka flowers.
highlights.security: Solid security thread, watermark with electrotype, Rapid HD® microlenses, and UV/phosphorescent technology.

title: 20.000 Rupiah
description: Dr. G. S. S. J. Ratulangi, arms.
body:
The 20,000 Rupiah note was introduced by Bank Indonesia on 18 August 2022 as part of the seven-denomination 2022 Rupiah series, unveiled around Indonesia's 77th Independence Day. The obverse features Dr. Gerungan Saul Samuel Jacob Ratulangi, national hero and first governor of Sulawesi, alongside the coat of arms of Indonesia, an outline of the archipelago, and the black orchid (Coelogyne pandurata). The reverse depicts a Gong dancer, scenery of the Derawan Islands in East Kalimantan, and repeated black orchid motifs.

The banknote measures 141 × 65 mm in predominantly green tones. Security elements include a windowed security thread with demetalized denomination marking, an embedded watermark with electrotype, microlenses, and optically variable ink with dynamic motion effects visible when the note is tilted. The 2022-series notes carry date and signature variants through 2026.

highlights.reason: Part of Bank Indonesia's 2022 Rupiah series honoring national heroes, with date variants through 2026.
highlights.design: Front: Dr. G. S. S. J. Ratulangi with arms and the black orchid; back: Gong dancer and Derawan Islands scenery.
highlights.security: Windowed demetalized security thread, electrotype watermark, microlenses, and optically variable ink with motion effects.

title: 100.000 Rupiah
body:
The 100,000 Rupiah banknote from the 2022 Bank Indonesia series honors Indonesia's first president Sukarno (Ir. Soekarno) and first vice president Mohammad Hatta, co-founding leaders of the Indonesian republic. The obverse displays their portraits side-by-side alongside the national coat of arms, a map of Indonesia, and moth orchid flowers. The reverse depicts three iconic elements: the Tari Topeng Betawi (a masked dance from the Betawi people of Jakarta), Raja Ampat island scenery, and moon orchid flowers (Phalaenopsis amabilis), showcasing Indonesia's cultural and natural diversity.

Measuring 151 mm × 66 mm, the note displays red as its dominant color with multicolor designs throughout. Issued on 17 August 2022, it is signed by Governor Perry Warjiyo and Finance Minister Sri Mulyani Indrawati. The 2022 series features innovative security enhancements including Rapid HD® detect micro lenses embedded in kawung batik motifs and the Bank Indonesia logo, plus OVMI Spark® technology with dynamic motion effects in the orchid flower designs.

Security features include a windowed security thread with demetallized "BI 100000" text, watermark of Wage Rudolf Soepratman and electrotype logo, bank logo registration device, and comprehensive ultraviolet and phosphorescent technology across all denominations.

highlights.reason: Honors Sukarno and Mohammad Hatta, Indonesia's founding leaders and architects of independence and unity.
highlights.design: Reverse features Tari Topeng Betawi masked dance, Raja Ampat island landscape, and moon orchid (Phalaenopsis) flowers.
highlights.security: Windowed security thread with demetallized text, Rapid HD® microlenses with motion effects, OVMI Spark® in flowers, and UV/phosphorescent elements.

title: 5 Dinars
description: King Abdullah I Ibn Hussain, Khazne al-Firaun (Treasury of the Pharao), Petra.
body:
The Central Bank of Jordan 5 Dinars banknote, issued in 2022 (1443H), honors King Abdullah I Ibn Hussain and features the Treasury of Petra (Al-Khazneh), one of the most iconic archaeological sites in the region. The front displays a portrait of King Abdullah with the rock-cut architecture of the Treasury against an orange, red, and brown color scheme. The reverse depicts a camel caravan flanking the pediment of the Treasury, complementing the archaeological theme and representing Jordan's cultural heritage and historical significance.

The note incorporates advanced security features reflecting modern banknote printing standards, including both solid and windowed security threads. The windowed security thread features demetalalized text reading "CBJ 5". A watermark of King Abdullah Bin Al-Hussein appears throughout the note, along with electrotype Arabic text for additional authentication. Registration devices in the form of crowns are positioned on both obverse and reverse, while an Optically Variable Ink (OVI) security patch enhances protection against counterfeiting.

The 5 Dinars denomination remains a standard circulation note in Jordan's currency system and reflects the Central Bank's commitment to security and artistic representation of the nation's heritage through numismatic design.

highlights.reason: Features King Abdullah I Ibn Hussain with the Treasury of Petra, a UNESCO World Heritage site and symbol of Jordan's historical and cultural importance.
highlights.design: Front: king portrait with rock-cut Treasury architecture on orange-red-brown background; reverse: camel caravan and Treasury pediment with crown registration devices.
highlights.security: Includes solid and windowed security threads, watermark of king, electrotype Arabic text, OVI patch, and crown registration devices for comprehensive counterfeiting protection.

title: 1.000 Piso
body:
The Philippine 1000 Piso polymer banknote, first issued in April 2022 and continuing through 2025, features two national symbols: the Philippine eagle (Pithecophaga jefferyi) and the Sampaguita flower (Jasminum sambac). The Philippine eagle represents Filipino uniqueness, strength, power, love of freedom, and vision for the nation's future. The Sampaguita symbolizes purity, simplicity, humility, and strength. The reverse side features the Tubbataha Reefs Natural Park, a UNESCO World Heritage Site recognized for its exceptional coral reef ecosystem and marine biodiversity, along with the South Sea Pearl, representing the Philippines' maritime heritage.

Launched on April 6, 2022, this was the first polymer banknote issued by the Bangko Sentral ng Pilipinas and earned the IBNS Banknote of the Year 2022 award. It continues to circulate alongside the new First Philippine Polymer (FPP) series introduced in December 2024, representing a significant advancement in Philippine banknote technology and durability, with polymer notes lasting up to 7.5 years compared to 1.5 years for paper notes.

highlights.reason: First Philippine polymer banknote honoring two national symbols (Philippine eagle and Sampaguita flower) to promote national identity and pride.

highlights.security: Incorporates 11 security features including rolling bar effect, tactile dots, embossed prints, sampaguita clear window, shadow thread, vertical clear window with metallic features, blue iridescent figure, and polymer substrate.

title: 100.000 Tögrög
description: Remark. 100th Anniversary of Peoples Revolution (1921-2021) and Tögög-Currency (1925-2025).
body:
The Mongolbank 100,000 Tögrög commemorative banknote was issued to celebrate the 100th anniversary of the People's Revolution, originally dated 2021 on the lower left of the reverse. Due to printing delays, the notes were officially introduced in December 2025, at which time they were overprinted with a red stamp on the lower center reverse reading "2025-12-09" to commemorate the 100th anniversary of the tögrög currency itself, which was introduced into circulation on 9 December 1925. This dual commemorative purpose unites Mongolia's political independence and monetary heritage.

The front of the note features Genghis Khan (Chinggis Khaan) as the primary portrait, along with the Soyombo national symbol and mountainous landscape imagery representing Mongolia's terrain and natural heritage. A warrior on horseback serves as a registration device. The reverse displays portraits of the First Seven leaders of the People's Revolution: Damdin Sukhbaatar, Dogsom Bodoo, Soli Danzan, Dansranbileg Dogsom, Darzav Losol, Damby Chagdarjav, and Khorloo Choibalsan. A map of Mongolia and silhouette of a warrior on horseback appear as additional design elements, rendered in orange, brown, red, and blue colors.

This commemorative issue marks a significant moment in Mongolia's financial history, bridging the centennial of its 1921 revolution with the centennial of its monetary system's establishment, making it a dual-purpose historical document.

highlights.design: Front: Genghis Khan portrait with Soyombo symbol and mountainous landscape; reverse: seven revolutionary leaders (Sukhbaatar, Bodoo, Danzan, and others) with Mongolia map and warrior on horseback.
highlights.security: Features multiple registration devices including warrior-on-horseback motifs, Soyombo symbol, and map elements as security design components; red overprint stamp dates (2021 and 2025-12-09) indicate authenticity.

title: 200 Riyals
description: King Abdulaziz Ibn Saud. 5th anniversary of launching Vision 2030 (2016-2021).
body:
The Saudi Central Bank (SAMA) issued the 200 Riyals commemorative banknote on 25 April 2021 to mark the fifth anniversary of the Kingdom's Vision 2030 initiative, launched in 2016. The front features a portrait of King Abdulaziz ibn Abdulrahman Al Saud, the founder of the modern Saudi state, alongside the distinctive 3D logo of Saudi Vision 2030 in a prominent position. The banknote is printed predominantly in gray tones with accents in other colors, presenting a modern aesthetic while maintaining the traditional elements of Saudi currency design.

The reverse side displays additional security and design elements, including a watermark and foil strip bearing the emblem of Saudi Arabia and the banknote value. The 200 Riyals denomination represents a major note within the sixth issue of Saudi Arabian banknotes, and its design incorporates the latest standards in banknote printing technology. The note carries the name and logo of the Saudi Central Bank (SAMA), the denomination in both Arabic letters and numbers, and all design elements are executed to the highest security standards.

This commemorative issue reflects Saudi Arabia's commitment to modernization and long-term development goals through Vision 2030, while celebrating the foundational leadership of King Abdulaziz and the nation's historical continuity.

highlights.security: Includes watermark and foil strip bearing Saudi emblem, latest banknote printing standards with multiple security layers, demetalalized elements, and denomination markings in Arabic and numbers.

title: 100 Somoni
body:
The National Bank of Tajikistan issued this regular circulation 100 Somoni banknote in 2021 as part of its standard currency series, continuing to circulate through 2025. This note differs from the commemorative 2025 issue (e44) and represents the standard design used for general economic circulation.

The obverse depicts Abu Ibrohim Ismoili ibni Ahmad Somonī, the founder of the Samanid state and historical emir of Transoxiana and Khorasan during the 9th century. The design includes the iconic Samanid Mausoleum and the tomb of Ismoili Somoni located in Bukhara, Uzbekistan.

The reverse features the Navruz Palace (Kohi Navruz), a significant architectural landmark in Dushanbe, alongside the national flag of Tajikistan. This represented a design change from earlier issues that featured the Presidential Palace.

Manufactured by Giesecke & Devrient, this 156 × 65 mm paper banknote is signed by national officials and incorporates a holographic stripe featuring Ismoil Somoni, the denomination, and the national coat of arms alongside a color-changing windowed security thread with demetalized elements.

highlights.reason: Regular circulation banknote distinguishable from the 2025 commemorative issue by its standard design and 2021 date.
highlights.design: Features Ismoili Somoni and his mausoleum on the obverse; Navruz Palace (replacing Presidential Palace from earlier versions) on the reverse.
highlights.security: Holographic stripe with Ismoil Somoni, denomination, and coat of arms; color-changing windowed security thread with demetalized elements.

title: 50 Dirhams
description: Seven founding fathers with flag after signing the union document.
body:
The Central Bank of the United Arab Emirates issued this polymer commemorative 50 Dirham banknote on 7 December 2021, marking the UAE's Golden Jubilee — the 50th anniversary of the founding union of 2 December 1971. The obverse features the seven founding fathers holding the national flag immediately after signing the union document, an iconic moment in the nation's history.

The reverse displays Sheikh Zayed bin Sultan Al Nahyan, founder of the union, signing the historic agreement, with the Etihad Museum in the background, together with the Martyrs' Memorial (Wahat Al Karama).

The note was the UAE's first polymer banknote, printed on De La Rue's SAFEGUARD substrate by Oumolat Security Printing in the UAE. With a violet and blue color scheme and measuring 151 × 64 mm, it incorporates a KINEGRAM COLORS multicolored foil security stripe visible on both sides and Braille embossing for accessibility. It remains in circulation alongside the regular denominations.

highlights.design: Front: the seven founding fathers with the flag after signing the union document; back: Sheikh Zayed signing the agreement with the Etihad Museum.
highlights.security: De La Rue SAFEGUARD polymer with KINEGRAM COLORS foil stripe on both sides and Braille embossing.
